([天岩戸](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%B2%A9%E6%88%B8 "wiktionary:天岩戸"), lit. 'Heavenly Rock Cave') – In Shinto, Amano-Iwato is the name of the [cave](/wiki/Cave "Cave") where Amaterasu fled after the violent actions of Susanoo caused the death of one of her weavers. Thus, the land was deprived of light, and *mononoke* from hell were free to roam the lands and wreak havoc. It took the other *kami* to lure her out again, restoring the sun to the world.

([天津甕星](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F "wiktionary:天津甕星"), lit. 'Dread Star of Heaven' or 'August Star of Heaven'), also-known-as *Ame-no-kagase-o* ([天香香背男](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%A6%99%E9%A6%99%E8%83%8C%E7%94%B7 "wiktionary:天香香背男")) – Originally a rebellious Shinto god who would not submit to the will of the other *[Amatsukami](/wiki/Amatsukami "Amatsukami")*. Under [Chinese Buddhist](/wiki/Chinese_Buddhism "Chinese Buddhism") influence,[[2]](#cite_note-2) the god was identified with [Myōken](/wiki/My%C5%8Dken "Myōken"), either as the [Pole Star](/wiki/Pole_Star "Pole Star") or [Venus](/wiki/Venus "Venus"), before being combined with the god of all stars, *Ama-no-mi-naka-nushi* ([天之御中主神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%B9%8B%E5%BE%A1%E4%B8%AD%E4%B8%BB%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天之御中主神"), lit. 'Divine Lord of the Middle-Heavens'). In some versions, Amatsu-Mikaboshi was born from the blood of Kagutsuchi spilt by Izanagi, after Kagutsuchi's birth. Amatsu-Mikaboshi is mentioned in passing in the *Nihon Shoki* as being subdued by [Takemikazuchi](/wiki/Takemikazuchi "Takemikazuchi"), during the latter's conquest of the land of *[Izumo](/wiki/Izumo_Province "Izumo Province")*.

([天逆毎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%80%86%E6%AF%8E "wiktionary:天逆毎"), lit. 'Opposing Heaven') – A goddess mentioned in the *[Kujiki](/wiki/Kujiki "Kujiki")*, which states that she originated when Susanoo let his *ara-mitama* build up inside him until he vomited

## A

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: A")]

* ***[Akabeko](/wiki/Akabeko "Akabeko")* ([赤べこ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%B5%A4%E3%81%B9%E3%81%93 "wiktionary:赤べこ"), lit. 'red cow')** – A red [papier-mâché](/wiki/Papier-m%C3%A2ch%C3%A9 "Papier-mâché") cow [bobblehead](/wiki/Bobblehead "Bobblehead") toy; a kind of *engimono* and an *[omiyage](/wiki/Omiyage "Omiyage")* (a regional souvenir in Japan) that is considered symbolic of [Aizu](/wiki/Aizu "Aizu").
* ***Akomeôgi* ([衵扇](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A1%B5%E6%89%87 "wiktionary:衵扇"), lit. 'chemise fan')** – A type of fan held by aristocratic women of the Heian period when formally dressed; it is brightly painted with [tassels](/wiki/Tassels "Tassels") and streamers on the ends. Held today in Shinto by a *miko* in formal costume for festivals. See also *hiôgi*.
* ***Aku* ([悪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%AA "wiktionary:悪"), lit. 'evil')** – The term's meaning is not limited to moral evil, and includes misfortune, inferiority and unhappiness.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Akuma](/wiki/Akuma_(folklore) "Akuma (folklore)")* ([悪魔](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%AA%E9%AD%94 "wiktionary:悪魔"), lit. 'devil')** – A malevolent fire spirit, demon or devil.
* ***Akuru* ([悪樓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%AA%E6%A8%93 "wiktionary:悪樓"), lit. 'evil tower')** – Also known as the *Akujin*, the *Kibi-no-Ananowatari-no-Kami* and as the *Anato-no-Kami*, *Akuru* is a malevolent *kami* that is mentioned in the *Keikoki* (records regarding the time of the Emperor Keiko), the *Nihonshoki* (Chronicles of Japan), and the *Kojiki* (The Records of Ancient Matters).
* ***Akuryō* ([悪霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%AA%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:悪霊"), lit. 'evil spirit')** – A [malevolent spirit](/wiki/Malevolent_spirit "Malevolent spirit"), demon or devil.
* ***Akuryō Taisan* ([悪霊退散](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%AA%E9%9C%8A%E9%80%80%E6%95%A3 "wiktionary:悪霊退散"))** – A spell or command to dispel a malevolent spirit, demon, or devil. One of the earliest uses of this phrase can be seen in *[Dōjōji](/wiki/D%C5%8Dj%C5%8Dji_(Noh_play) "Dōjōji (Noh play)")*.
* ***[Ama](/wiki/Deva_(Buddhism) "Deva (Buddhism)")* ([天](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9 "wiktionary:天"), lit. 'heaven(ly)')** – The divine/deva realm of incarnation, the highest realm on the Wheel of Reincarnation.
* ***[Amagatsu](/wiki/H%C5%8Dko_(doll)#Amagatsu "Hōko (doll)")* ([天児](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%85%90 "wiktionary:天児"), lit. 'heaven(ly) newborn')** – See *hōko*.
* ***Amakudari* ([天降](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%99%8D "wiktionary:天降") & [天下り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%B8%8B%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:天下り"), lit. 'heavenly descent')** – A (living) being who is an incarnation of a divine being; an [avatar](/wiki/Avatar "Avatar").
* **[Amano-Iwato](/wiki/Amano-Iwato "Amano-Iwato")** ([天岩戸](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%B2%A9%E6%88%B8 "wiktionary:天岩戸"), lit. 'Heavenly Rock Cave') – In Shinto, Amano-Iwato is the name of the [cave](/wiki/Cave "Cave") where Amaterasu fled after the violent actions of Susanoo caused the death of one of her weavers. Thus, the land was deprived of light, and *mononoke* from hell were free to roam the lands and wreak havoc. It took the other *kami* to lure her out again, restoring the sun to the world.
* **[Amatsu-Mikaboshi](/wiki/Amatsu-Mikaboshi "Amatsu-Mikaboshi")** ([天津甕星](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F "wiktionary:天津甕星"), lit. 'Dread Star of Heaven' or 'August Star of Heaven'), also-known-as *Ame-no-kagase-o* ([天香香背男](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%A6%99%E9%A6%99%E8%83%8C%E7%94%B7 "wiktionary:天香香背男")) – Originally a rebellious Shinto god who would not submit to the will of the other *[Amatsukami](/wiki/Amatsukami "Amatsukami")*. Under [Chinese Buddhist](/wiki/Chinese_Buddhism "Chinese Buddhism") influence,[[2]](#cite_note-2) the god was identified with [Myōken](/wiki/My%C5%8Dken "Myōken"), either as the [Pole Star](/wiki/Pole_Star "Pole Star") or [Venus](/wiki/Venus "Venus"), before being combined with the god of all stars, *Ama-no-mi-naka-nushi* ([天之御中主神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%B9%8B%E5%BE%A1%E4%B8%AD%E4%B8%BB%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天之御中主神"), lit. 'Divine Lord of the Middle-Heavens'). In some versions, Amatsu-Mikaboshi was born from the blood of Kagutsuchi spilt by Izanagi, after Kagutsuchi's birth. Amatsu-Mikaboshi is mentioned in passing in the *Nihon Shoki* as being subdued by [Takemikazuchi](/wiki/Takemikazuchi "Takemikazuchi"), during the latter's conquest of the land of *[Izumo](/wiki/Izumo_Province "Izumo Province")*.
* ***[Amanojaku](/wiki/Amanojaku "Amanojaku")* ([天の邪鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E3%81%AE%E9%82%AA%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:天の邪鬼"), lit. 'malevolent spirit from heaven')** – An *oni*-like creature in Japanese folklore; the *amanojaku* is thought to be able to provoke a person's darkest desires, and can instigate them into perpetrating wicked deeds. Similar to Amanozako. See also *jaki* and *jama*.
* ***[Ama-no-uki-hashi](/wiki/Takamagahara "Takamagahara")* ([天之浮橋](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%B9%8B%E6%B5%AE%E6%A9%8B "wiktionary:天之浮橋"), lit. 'floating bridge of heaven')** – In Shinto, it is the bridge connecting Earth and Takamagahara.
* **[Amanozako](/wiki/Amanozako "Amanozako")** ([天逆毎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%80%86%E6%AF%8E "wiktionary:天逆毎"), lit. 'Opposing Heaven') – A goddess mentioned in the *[Kujiki](/wiki/Kujiki "Kujiki")*, which states that she originated when Susanoo let his *ara-mitama* build up inside him until he vomited her out. Similar to *amanojaku*.
* **[Amaterasu](/wiki/Amaterasu "Amaterasu") Ōmikami** ([天照大神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%85%A7%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天照大神"), [天照大御神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%85%A7%E5%A4%A7%E5%BE%A1%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天照大御神") or [天照皇大神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%85%A7%E7%9A%87%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天照皇大神"), lit. 'Heaven(ly) Illumination Great Honourable Imperial Divinity') – The Shinto sun goddess, tutelary *kami* and ancestor of the [Emperor](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan"), enshrined at [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine").[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Amatsukami](/wiki/Amatsukami "Amatsukami")* ([天津神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天津神"), lit. 'Heavenly Deities')** – *Kami* from Takamagahara.
* ***Amatsu tsumi* ([天津罪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%BD%AA "wiktionary:天津罪") / [天つ罪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E3%81%A4%E7%BD%AA "wiktionary:天つ罪"), lit. 'Crimes in Heaven')** – A term for tsumi specifically committed against heaven. For example, the crimes committed by Susanoo-no-Mikoto against Amaterasu are considered *amatsu tsumi*. The corresponding concept to *amatsu tsumi* is *kunitsu tsumi*.
* ***[Ame-no-Nuhoko](/wiki/Amenonuhoko "Amenonuhoko")* ([天沼矛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B2%BC%E7%9F%9B "wiktionary:天沼矛"), [天之瓊矛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%B9%8B%E7%93%8A%E7%9F%9B "wiktionary:天之瓊矛") or [天瓊戈](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%93%8A%E6%88%88 "wiktionary:天瓊戈"), lit. 'Jewel(ed) Spear of Heaven')** – The spear used by [Izanagi](/wiki/Izanagi "Izanagi") and [Izanami](/wiki/Izanami "Izanami") to raise the primordial landmass, *Onogoro-shima*, from the sea; it is often depicted as a *naginata*.
* ***[Ame-no-Uzume](/wiki/Ame-no-Uzume "Ame-no-Uzume")* ([天宇受売命](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%AE%87%E5%8F%97%E5%A3%B2%E5%91%BD "wiktionary:天宇受売命") or [天鈿女命](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E9%88%BF%E5%A5%B3%E5%91%BD "wiktionary:天鈿女命"), lit. 'Shining Heavenly Sky Goddess')** – The Shinto goddess of the dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts, and the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. See also Otafuku.
* ***[An](/wiki/An_(Shinto) "An (Shinto)")\** ([案](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A1%88 "wiktionary:案"), lit. 'table' or 'platform')** – A small portable table or platform used during Shinto ceremonies to bear offerings. It may have four, eight or sixteen legs.
* ***Ano-Yo* ([あの世](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%82%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%96 "wiktionary:あの世"), lit. 'that world')** – See *seishinkai*.
* ***Anzen* ([安全](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%89%E5%85%A8 "wiktionary:安全"), lit. 'safety' or 'security')** – A type of *omamori*, specifically for safety, particularly safety at work, frequently requested from a *kami*, and in fact corporations often have a [tutelary shrine](/wiki/Chinjusha "Chinjusha") specifically to ensure their business prospers.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Aoi Matsuri](/wiki/Aoi_Matsuri "Aoi Matsuri")* ([葵祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%91%B5%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:葵祭"), lit. '[Hollyhock](/wiki/Hollyhock "Hollyhock") Festival')** – One of the three main annual festivals held in Kyoto, Japan (the other two being the Festival of the Ages (Jidai Matsuri) and the Gion Festival). It is a festival of the two *kamo* shrines in the north of the city: Shimogamo Shrine and Kamigamo Shrine. The festival may also be referred to as the *Kamo* Festival. It is held on 15 May of each year.
* ***[Ara-mitama](/wiki/Mitama "Mitama")* ([荒魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%8D%92%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:荒魂"), lit. 'Wild Soul')** – The rough and violent side of the *mitama*.[[3]](#cite_note-ara-3) The *ara-mitama* is associated with the colours black and purple, and the cardinal direction north. *Ama-no-Zako* is actually the incarnated *ara-mitama* of [Susanoo-no-Mikoto](/wiki/Susanoo-no-Mikoto "Susanoo-no-Mikoto").
* ***Aruki miko* ([歩き巫女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%AD%A9%E3%81%8D%E5%B7%AB%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:歩き巫女"), lit. 'wandering shrine maiden')** – An itinerant *miko* that is not in service to a particular shrine, and wanders throughout the country performing services where needed, and living off-of charity.
* ***[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni](/wiki/Ashihara_no_Nakatsukuni "Ashihara no Nakatsukuni")* ([葦原の中つ国](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%91%A6%E5%8E%9F%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%AD%E3%81%A4%E5%9B%BD "wiktionary:葦原の中つ国"), lit. The "Middle country of [reed beds](/wiki/Reed_bed "Reed bed")")** – In Shinto, this term is applied to the plane of existence that exists between *Takamagahara* and *Yomi*, or the realm of the living. The term became another word for the country or the location of Japan itself. The term can be used interchangeably with *[Toyoashihara no Nakatsukuni](/wiki/Names_of_Japan#Classical "Names of Japan")*.
* ***[A-un](/wiki/A-un "A-un")* ([阿吽](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%98%BF%E5%90%BD "wiktionary:阿吽"), lit. '[Om](/wiki/Om "Om")')** – In *Shinto*-*Buddhism*, *a-un* is the transliteration in Japanese of the two syllables "a" and "hūṃ", written in [Devanagari](/wiki/Devanagari "Devanagari") as अहूँ (the syllable, *[Om](/wiki/Om "Om")*). See also *[Nio](/wiki/Nio_(Buddhism) "Nio (Buddhism)")* and *[Gozu and Mezu](/wiki/Ox-Head_and_Horse-Face "Ox-Head and Horse-Face")*.
* ***[Ayakashi](/wiki/Ayakashi_(y%C5%8Dkai) "Ayakashi (yōkai)")* ([妖怪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%96%E6%80%AA "wiktionary:妖怪"), lit. 'Strange, Unusual, Supernatural, Paranormal, Extraordinary')** – An umbrella term that covers ghosts, phantoms, phantasms, apparitions and illusions, goblins, monsters, demons, devils and any kind of supernatural beasts and beings; the corporeal and the incorporeal; real or fantasy; *ayakashi* is a term more specific for *yōkai* that appear above the surface of some body of water. See also *rinka*, [shiranui](/wiki/Shiranui_(optical_phenomenon) "Shiranui (optical phenomenon)"), *[will-o'-the-wisp](/wiki/Will-o%27-the-wisp "Will-o'-the-wisp"), and* *[St. Elmo's fire](/wiki/St._Elmo%27s_fire "St. Elmo's fire")*.
* ***[Azusa yumi](/wiki/Azusa_yumi "Azusa yumi")* ([梓弓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A2%93%E5%BC%93 "wiktionary:梓弓"), lit. '[cherry birch](/wiki/Betula_grossa "Betula grossa") bow')** – a sacred bow used in certain Shinto rituals in Japan, as well as a Japanese musical bow; made from the wood of the Japanese cherry birch tree (*Betula grossa*). Playing an *azusa yumi* forms part of some Shinto rituals; in Japan, it is universally believed that merely the twanging of the bowstring will frighten ghosts and evil spirits away from a house.

## B

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: B")]

* ***[Bakemono](/wiki/Bakemono "Bakemono")* ([化け物](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8C%96%E3%81%91%E7%89%A9 "wiktionary:化け物"), lit. 'transforming thing')** – A monstrous apparition; a monster or ghost.
* ***Banbutsu* ([万物](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%87%E7%89%A9 "wiktionary:万物"), lit. 'ten thousand things')** – A term used to refer to the whole world.
* ***Banshō* ([万象](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%87%E8%B1%A1 "wiktionary:万象"), lit. 'ten thousand likenesses')** – A term describing all things.
* ***Bekkū *or* betsugū* ([別宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%A5%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:別宮"), lit. 'separate [temple](/wiki/Temple "Temple")/[shrine](/wiki/Shrine "Shrine")')** – A [subsidiary](/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") shrine next to the *honden*, which may however enshrine an equally important *kami*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Benzaiten](/wiki/Benzaiten "Benzaiten")** ([弁才天](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%81%E6%89%8D%E5%A4%A9 "wiktionary:弁才天"), [弁財天](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%81%E8%B2%A1%E5%A4%A9 "wiktionary:弁財天"), lit. '[Heavenly](/wiki/Heaven "Heaven")-[Happy](/wiki/Happy "Happy") Talents') – Originally a Vedic goddess, [Sarasvati](/wiki/Sarasvati "Sarasvati"), Benzaiten is a [syncretic](/wiki/Syncretism "Syncretism") goddess and a member of the [Seven Lucky Gods](/wiki/Seven_Lucky_Gods "Seven Lucky Gods"). She is also syncretized with *Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto* ([市杵島姫命](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B8%82%E6%9D%B5%E5%B3%B6%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD "wiktionary:市杵島姫命"), lit. 'Female [goddess] of the Island of Worship').[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Bettō](/wiki/Bett%C5%8D "Bettō")* ([別当](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%A5%E5%BD%93 "wiktionary:別当"), lit. 'acting-division head')** – Before the shinbutsu bunri, when the [Meiji period](/wiki/Meiji_period "Meiji period") law forbade the mixing of Shinto and [Buddhism](/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism"), a *bettō* was a monk who performed Buddhist rites at a Shinto shrine.
* **[Bishamonten](/wiki/Vai%C5%9Brava%E1%B9%87a "Vaiśravaṇa")** – Syncretic deity of Buddhist origin part of the Seven Lucky Gods.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) A symbol of authority, he protects warriors.
* ***[Bon Matsuri](/wiki/Bon_Festival "Bon Festival")* ([盆](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9B%86 "wiktionary:盆"), lit. '[Votive offering](/wiki/Votive_offering "Votive offering") [Festival](/wiki/Festival "Festival")')** – A festival celebrated around July 15 in order to console the spirits of the dead. In theory a Buddhist festival, but in practice an ancestor and family festival part of Shinto.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Bonshō](/wiki/Bonsh%C5%8D "Bonshō")* ([梵鐘](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A2%B5%E9%90%98 "wiktionary:梵鐘"), lit. 'Buddhist temple bell')** – Large bells found in Buddhist temples throughout Japan, used to summon the monks to prayer and to demarcate periods of time. Instead of containing a clapper, *bonshō* are struck from the outside using either a handheld mallet or a beam suspended on ropes. See also *suzu*, *kagura suzu*, *rin*, and *dōtaku*.
* ***[Bosatsu](/wiki/Bosatsu "Bosatsu")* ([菩薩](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%8F%A9%E8%96%A9 "wiktionary:菩薩"))** – A *[bodhisattva](/wiki/Bodhisattva "Bodhisattva")*. Term of Buddhist origin which is often used for deities of mixed Buddhist/Shinto ancestry such as Benzaiten and *[jizō](/wiki/Jiz%C5%8D "Jizō")*, *kami* like Hachiman, and deified human beings like [Tokugawa Ieyasu](/wiki/Tokugawa_Ieyasu "Tokugawa Ieyasu").[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Bōrei](/wiki/Y%C5%ABrei "Yūrei")* ([亡霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BA%A1%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:亡霊"), lit. 'deceased spirit')** – A term for a ghost; a type of *yūrei*, but one whose identity (and grudge) is unknown.
* ***[Bokusen](/wiki/Divination "Divination")* ([卜占](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%9C%E5%8D%A0 "wiktionary:卜占"), lit. 'divination, scrying, fortune-telling')** – The act of divining; foreseeing or a foretelling of future events. See also *futomani* and *ukehi*.
* ***Buden* ([舞殿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%88%9E%E6%AE%BF "wiktionary:舞殿"), lit. 'dance hall')** – Another word for a *kaguraden*, a pavilion or stage dedicated to the performing of the *kagura*.
* ***[Bunrei](/wiki/Bunrei "Bunrei")* ([分霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%86%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:分霊"), lit. 'soul division')** – A process of division of a *kami* producing two complete copies of the original, one of which is then transferred to a new shrine by a process called *[kanjō](/wiki/Kanj%C5%8D "Kanjō")*.
* ***[Bunsha](/wiki/Shinto_shrine#Famous_shrines_and_shrine_networks "Shinto shrine")* ([分社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%86%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:分社"), lit. 'shrine division')** – A shrine that is a part of a network headed by a more famous shrine, from whence its *kami* was transferred by *kanjō*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Busshitsukai](/wiki/Physical_world "Physical world")* ([物質界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%89%A9%E8%B3%AA%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:物質界"), lit. 'material world')** – The corporeal world.

## C

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: C")]

* ***[Chi](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([智](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%99%BA "wiktionary:智"), lit. '[wisdom](/wiki/Wisdom "Wisdom"), [knowledge](/wiki/Knowledge "Knowledge"), [intelligence](/wiki/Intelligence "Intelligence")')** – One of the virtues of *[bushido](/wiki/Bushido "Bushido")*.
* ***[Chigi](/wiki/Chigi_(architecture) "Chigi (architecture)")\** ([千木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%83%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:千木"), lit. '[Thousand](/wiki/Thousand "Thousand") [Wood](/wiki/Wood "Wood")(en beams)')** – Forked decorations common at the ends of the roof of shrines.
* ***Chihara* ([襅](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A5%85 "wiktionary:襅"), [千早](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%83%E6%97%A9 "wiktionary:千早"), lit. 'finishing-touch robe')** – A type of ceremonial overcoat with a long white hem worn by a *Miko* in certain Shinto ceremonies; similar to a Kannushi's *Jōe* over-robes.
* ***[Chinja](/wiki/Chinjusha "Chinjusha")* (鎮社, lit. 'garrison shrine')** – The tutelary *kami* or tutelary shrine of a certain area or [Buddhist temple](/wiki/Buddhist_temples_in_Japan "Buddhist temples in Japan"); see also *chinjusha*.
* ***[Chinjusha](/wiki/Chinjusha "Chinjusha")\** ([鎮守社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8E%AE%E5%AE%88%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:鎮守社"), lit. 'garrison protector shrine')** – a small shrine dedicated to the tutelary *kami* of an area or building[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) (see also *Chinju*).
* ***[Chitose ame](/wiki/Shichi-Go-San#Chitose_ame "Shichi-Go-San")* ([千歳飴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%83%E6%AD%B3%E9%A3%B4 "wiktionary:千歳飴"), lit. 'thousand-year candy')** – Long, thin sticks of red-and-white candy—which symbolizes healthy growth and longevity—sold at festivals for children, specifically for *Shichi-Go-San*. *Chitose ame* is given in a bag decorated with a crane and a turtle—which represent long life in Japan. *Chitose ame* is wrapped in thin, clear, and edible rice paper film that resembles plastic.
* ***[Chōchin](/wiki/Traditional_lighting_equipment_of_Japan#Chōchin "Traditional lighting equipment of Japan")* ([提灯](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%8F%90%E7%81%AF "wiktionary:提灯"), lit. 'portable lantern')** – Paper lanterns always present at Shinto festivals.
* ***[Chōchō](/wiki/Butterfly#In_mythology_and_folklore "Butterfly")* ([蝶](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%9D%B6 "wiktionary:蝶"), lit. 'butterfly')** – Butterflies native to Japan and to Japanese culture. The *chōchō* is also featured among *engimono* (above).It is seen as lucky, especially if seen in pairs; if a symbol contains two butterflies dancing around each other, it is a symbol of marital happiness.
* ***[Chikushō](/wiki/Animals_in_Buddhism "Animals in Buddhism")* ([畜生](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%95%9C%E7%94%9F "wiktionary:畜生"), lit. 'animal/livestock')** – The mortal, animal realm of incarnation, the third-lowest realm on the wheel of reincarnation. See also *rinne*.
* ***[Chōzubachi](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dzubachi "Chōzubachi")* ([手水鉢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E9%89%A2 "wiktionary:手水鉢"), lit. 'hand-washing earthenware basin')** – Usually made of stone, a *chōzubachi* is a water bowl, is a vessel used to rinse the hands in Japanese temples, shrines and gardens; see also *Chōzuya*.
* ***[Chōzuya](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dzuya "Chōzuya")* ([手水舎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E8%88%8E "wiktionary:手水舎"), lit. 'hand-washing pavilion')** – A Shinto water ablution pavilion for a ceremonial purification rite. The pavilion contains a large water-filled basin called a *chōzu-bachi*.
* ***Chinkon* ([鎮魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8E%AE%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:鎮魂"), lit. 'the calming of the spirits')** – A Shinto ritual performed for converting *ara-mitama* into *nigi-mitama*, quelling maleficent spirits, prevent misfortune and alleviate fear from events and circumstances that could not otherwise be explained; i.e. *Ara-mitama* that failed to achieve deification due to lack of sufficient veneration, or who lost their divinity following attrition of worshipers, became *yōkai*. See also; [Segaki](/wiki/Segaki "Segaki").
* ***Chinkonsei* ([鎮魂祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8E%AE%E9%AD%82%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:鎮魂祭"), lit. 'Calming-of-the Spirits Service' or '[Requiem](/wiki/Requiem "Requiem")')** – A *Shinto* *Matsuri* (a festival) performed for converting *ara-mitama* into *nigi-mitama*, quelling maleficent spirits, preventing misfortune and alleviating fear from events and circumstances that could not otherwise be explained.
* ***[Chūgi](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([忠義](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%A0%E7%BE%A9 "wiktionary:忠義"), lit. '[Duty](/wiki/Duty "Duty") and [Loyalty](/wiki/Loyalty "Loyalty")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.

## D

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: D")]

* ***Daijōsai* ([大嘗祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E5%98%97%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:大嘗祭"), lit. 'Great Tastes Festival')** – A ceremony marking the beginning of an emperor's reign in which he offers first fruits to ancestors, including Amaterasu.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Daikokuten](/wiki/Daikokuten "Daikokuten")** ([大黒天](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E9%BB%92%E5%A4%A9 "wiktionary:大黒天"), lit. 'Great Black Heavens') – A syncretic god, part of the Seven Lucky God fusing [Mahakala](/wiki/Mahakala "Mahakala"), and Ōkuninushi.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Daikyōkan Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([大叫喚地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E5%8F%AB%E5%96%9A%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:大叫喚地獄"), lit. 'Hell of Great Screaming')** – The fifth level of *Jigoku*; sinners who have committed murder (even the murder of small creatures such as insects), theft, degeneration, drunkenness, and lying are sent here.
* ***[Daishonetsu Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([大焦熱地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E7%84%A6%E7%86%B1%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:大焦熱地獄"), lit. 'Hell of Great Burning')** – The seventh level of *Jigoku*. Sinners who have committed murder (even the murder of small creatures such as insects), theft, degeneration, drunkenness, lying, blasphemy, and rape are sent here.
* ***[Den-den daiko](/wiki/Den-den_daiko "Den-den daiko")* ([でんでん太鼓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%A7%E3%82%93%E3%81%A7%E3%82%93%E5%A4%AA%E9%BC%93 "wiktionary:でんでん太鼓"))** – a Japanese hand-held [pellet drum](/wiki/Pellet_drum "Pellet drum") that is used in Shinto-Buddhist ceremonies.
* ***[Dōsojin](/wiki/D%C5%8Dsojin "Dōsojin")* ([道祖神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%81%93%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:道祖神"), lit. 'travelling guardian divinities')** – A group of [liminal](/wiki/Liminal_deity "Liminal deity") *kami* and Buddhist gods, protectors of roads, borders, boundaries and other places of transition.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **Dojin** ([土神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9C%9F%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:土神"), lit. 'earth god') – Another name for any Shinto earth deity.
* ***[Dōtaku](/wiki/D%C5%8Dtaku "Dōtaku")* ([銅鐸](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8A%85%E9%90%B8 "wiktionary:銅鐸"), lit. 'copper bell')** – Large Japanese bronze bells smelted from relatively thin bronze and richly decorated. See also *bonshō*, *rin*, and *suzu*.

## E

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: E")]

* ***[Ebisu](/wiki/Ebisu_(mythology) "Ebisu (mythology)")* ([恵比須](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88 "wiktionary:恵比須"), [恵比寿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E5%AF%BF "wiktionary:恵比寿"), [夷](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%B7 "wiktionary:夷"), [戎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%88%8E "wiktionary:戎"))** – The *kami* of prosperity found at both temples and shrines. One of the Seven Lucky Gods.
* ***Eboshi* ([烏帽子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%83%8F%E5%B8%BD%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:烏帽子"), lit. 'raven (black) hat')** – A type of [pointed hat](/wiki/Pointed_hat "Pointed hat") originally worn by Heian era aristocrats and samurai. Worn by *Kannushi* as formalwear for occasions such as festivals and weddings. See also *kanmuri* and *kazaori eboshi*.
* ***Eirei* ([英霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%8B%B1%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:英霊"))** – The spirit or ghost of a warrior/soldier who fell in battle.
* ***[Ema](/wiki/Ema_(Shinto) "Ema (Shinto)")\** ([絵馬](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B5%B5%E9%A6%AC "wiktionary:絵馬"), lit. 'picture horse')** – Small wooden plaques on which worshippers at shrines, as well as Buddhist temples, write their prayers or wishes.
* ***[Engimono](/wiki/Shinto#Engimono,_Ema,_divination,_and_amulets "Shinto")* ([縁起物](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B8%81%E8%B5%B7%E7%89%A9 "wiktionary:縁起物"))** – An [umbrella term](/wiki/Umbrella_term "Umbrella term") for [talismans](/wiki/Talisman "Talisman") and [good luck charms](/wiki/Good_luck_charm "Good luck charm") such as *[omamori](/wiki/Omamori "Omamori")* and *[ofuda](/wiki/Ofuda "Ofuda")*.

## F

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: F")]

* **[*Fuji-san*](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji") ([富士山](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%B1%B1 "wiktionary:富士山"))** – The most famous among Japan's three sacred mountains, the Three Spiritual Mountains (三霊山, *[Sanreizan](/wiki/Three_Holy_Mountains "Three Holy Mountains")*). Mt. Fuji is inhabited by a *kami* called [Konohanasakuya-hime](/wiki/Konohanasakuya-hime "Konohanasakuya-hime").[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Fūjin](/wiki/F%C5%ABjin "Fūjin")* ([風神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A2%A8%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:風神"))** – The *kami* of the winds who is the brother of Raijin; together, they are both said to be two of the many sons and other children of Izanagi and Izanami.
* **Restoration Shinto (復古神道, *[Fukko shintō](/wiki/Fukkoshinto "Fukkoshinto")*)** – A term synonymous with *[kokugaku](/wiki/Kokugaku "Kokugaku")*.
* ***[Futomani](/wiki/Futomani "Futomani")* (太占)** – A traditional Shinto system of divination, similar to the Chinese [oracle bone](/wiki/Oracle_bone "Oracle bone") technique, that uses stag bones.

## G

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: G")]

* ***[Gagaku](/wiki/Gagaku "Gagaku")* ([雅楽](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9B%85%E6%A5%BD "wiktionary:雅楽"), lit. 'elegant music')** – Ancient court music that was introduced into Japan with Buddhism from the Korean peninsula and China; now played for Shinto and Buddhist rituals and ceremonies.
* ***[Gaki](/wiki/Preta "Preta")* ([餓鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A4%93%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:餓鬼"), lit. 'hungry ghost')** – The "hungry ghost" realm of incarnation, the second-lowest realm on the wheel of reincarnation. See *rinne*.
* ***[Gehōbako](/wiki/Geh%C5%8Dbako "Gehōbako")* ([外法箱](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%96%E6%B3%95%E7%AE%B1 "wiktionary:外法箱"), lit. 'outer path box')** – A supernatural box that is used to hold Shinto paraphernalia, particular to a given *jinja*; such contents include dolls, animal and human skulls, and Shinto rosaries/prayer beads.
* ***[Gi](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([義](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BE%A9 "wiktionary:義"), lit. '[righteousness](/wiki/Righteousness "Righteousness")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Gogyo](/wiki/Gogyo "Gogyo")*** (五元) – The Five Elements.
* ***[Gion Matsuri](/wiki/Gion_Matsuri "Gion Matsuri")* ([祇園祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:祇園祭"))** – One of the three main annual festivals held in Kyoto, Japan (the other two being the *Aoi Matsuri* and the *Jidai Festival*). Gion Matsuri is one of the largest festivals in Japan for purification and pacification of disease-causing-entities. It takes place on 17 and 24 July.
* ***[Giri](/wiki/Giri_(Japanese) "Giri (Japanese)")* ([義理](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BE%A9%E7%90%86 "wiktionary:義理"), lit. '[duty](/wiki/Duty "Duty")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Go-hei](/wiki/Gohei "Gohei")\** ([御幣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E5%B9%A3 "wiktionary:御幣"), lit. 'great [wand](/wiki/Wand "Wand")')** – A wooden wand decorated with two *shide* and used in Shinto rituals as a *yorishiro*.
* ***Gokō* ([後光](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%8C%E5%85%89 "wiktionary:後光"), lit. 'background light')** – The Shinto-Buddhist equivalent of an [aureola](/wiki/Aureola "Aureola") or [halo](/wiki/Halo_(religious_iconography) "Halo (religious iconography)"). See also *tenne*.
* ***Gokuraku Jōdo* ([極楽浄土](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A5%B5%E6%A5%BD%E6%B5%84%E5%9C%9F "wiktionary:極楽浄土"), lit. 'paradise' or 'pure land')** – See *jōdo*.
* ***[Gongen](/wiki/Gongen "Gongen")* ([権現](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A8%A9%E7%8F%BE "wiktionary:権現"), lit. 'Current [Authority](/wiki/Authority "Authority"); [Incarnation](/wiki/Incarnation "Incarnation")')** – A Buddhist god that chooses to appear as a Japanese *kami* to take the Japanese to spiritual salvation, and a name sometimes used for shrines (e.g. "Tokusō Gongen") before the *shinbutsu bunri*.
* ***[Gongen-zukuri](/wiki/Gongen#Gongen-zukuri "Gongen")* ([権現造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A8%A9%E7%8F%BE%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:権現造"))** – A shrine structure in which the [*haiden*](/wiki/Haiden_(Shinto) "Haiden (Shinto)"), the *heiden* and the *[honden](/wiki/Honden "Honden")* are connected under the same roof in the shape of an H.\* See also *[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri](/wiki/Ishi-no-ma-zukuri "Ishi-no-ma-zukuri")*.
* ***[Goryō](/wiki/Gory%C5%8D "Goryō")* ([御霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:御霊"), lit. 'honourable soul')** – A soul, angry for having died violently or unhappy, which needs to be pacified through Buddhist rites or enshrinement, like [Sugawara no Michizane](/wiki/Sugawara_no_Michizane "Sugawara no Michizane");[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) vengeful Japanese ghosts from the aristocratic classes, especially those who have been martyred.
* ***[Gosekku](/wiki/Gosekku "Gosekku")* ([五節句](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BA%94%E7%AF%80%E5%8F%A5 "wiktionary:五節句"), lit. 'five seasonal festivals')** – The five annual cultural festivals that were traditionally held in the [Japanese imperial court](/wiki/Japanese_imperial_court "Japanese imperial court"). The *Gosekku* were originally adapted from Chinese practices and first celebrated in Japan in the *Nara* period in the 8th–10th centuries CE. The festivals were held until the beginning of the Meiji era.
* ***Gozu-tennō* ([牛頭天王](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%89%9B%E9%A0%AD%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B "wiktionary:牛頭天王"))** – Buddhist name of Susanoo, considered an avatar of *[Bhaisajyaguru](/wiki/Bhaisajyaguru "Bhaisajyaguru")*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Gozu and Mezu](/wiki/Ox-Head_and_Horse-Face#Japanese_mythology "Ox-Head and Horse-Face")** ([牛頭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%89%9B%E9%A0%AD "wiktionary:牛頭"), lit. 'Ox-head', and [馬頭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A6%AC%E9%A0%AD "wiktionary:馬頭"), lit. 'Horse-head') – In Shinto-Buddhism, Gozu and Mezu are the Japanese names for Niútóu and Mǎmiàn, two guardians of the underworld in Chinese and Shinto-Buddhist mythology. Both have the bodies of men, but Gozu has the head of an ox while Mezu has the face of a horse. They are the first beings a dead soul encounters upon entering *Jigoku*; in many stories they directly escort the newly dead to the underworld.
* ***-gū* ([宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:宮"), lit. 'shrine, temple')** – A suffix of some shrine names indicating it enshrines a member of the imperial family.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) [Hachiman-gū shrines](/wiki/Hachiman_shrine "Hachiman shrine"), for instance, enshrine [Emperor Ōjin](/wiki/Emperor_%C5%8Cjin "Emperor Ōjin").
* ***[Gunbai](/wiki/Gunbai "Gunbai")* ([軍配](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%BB%8D%E9%85%8D "wiktionary:軍配"), lit. 'army positioning')** – An item associated with leadership and ceremonial significance, back in Ancient Japan; wielded by royalty, aristocracy, *[daimyo](/wiki/Daimyo "Daimyo")*, military leaders, and *kannushi*; nowadays used by umpires in sumo.

## H

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: H")]

* ***[Hachiman](/wiki/Hachiman "Hachiman")* ([八幡神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:八幡神"), lit. 'Eight Banners Divinity')** – A syncretic tutelary *kami* of the warrior class. First enshrined at [Usa Hachiman-gū](/wiki/Usa_Hachiman-g%C5%AB "Usa Hachiman-gū"), it consists of three separate figures: [Emperor Ōjin](/wiki/Emperor_%C5%8Cjin "Emperor Ōjin"), his mother, and his wife Himegami.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Hachiman-zukuri](/wiki/Hachiman-zukuri "Hachiman-zukuri")* ([八幡造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:八幡造"), lit. 'Hachiman architecture')** – Shinto architectural style in which two parallel structures with gabled roofs are connecting on the non-gabled side forming a single building which, when seen from the side, gives the impression of two.[[4]](#cite_note-jaha-4)
* ***[Hagoromo](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin")*** ([羽衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BE%BD%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:羽衣"), lit. 'Feather[ed] Raiment') – The [stole-like](/wiki/Stole_(vestment) "Stole (vestment)"), feathered, heavenly [kimono](/wiki/Kimono "Kimono") or mantle of *[tennin](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin")* (see below), spiritual beings found in Japanese *Shinto-Buddhism*; *hagoromo* allowed the *tennin* wearing them to fly.
* ***[Haiden](/wiki/Haiden_(Shinto) "Haiden (Shinto)")* ([拝殿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%8B%9D%E6%AE%BF "wiktionary:拝殿"), lit. 'Hall of Prayer')** – A shrine building dedicated to prayer, and the only one of a shrine open to [laity](/wiki/Laity "Laity").
* ***[Hakama](/wiki/Hakama "Hakama")* ([袴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A2%B4 "wiktionary:袴"))** – A type of traditional Japanese clothing; originally inspired from *kù* ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 裤; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 褲), trousers used by the Chinese imperial court in the Sui and Tang dynasties. This style was adopted by the Japanese in the form of the *hakama*, beginning in the sixth century.
* ***[Haku](/wiki/Hun_and_po "Hun and po")* ([魄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%84 "wiktionary:魄"))** – Stemming from [Taoism](/wiki/Taoism "Taoism"), *haku* is the complement of *kon*, and is the [life force](/wiki/Vitalism "Vitalism") that is attached to the body, and returns to the earth after death.
* ***Hakurei* ([魄霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%84%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:魄霊"))** – The soul or spirit of a person who has usually just died.
* **[Hakusan](/wiki/Mount_Haku "Mount Haku")** – Collective name given to three mountains worshiped as *kami* and sacred to the Shugendō.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Hakusan shrines are common all over Japan.
* ***[Hamaya](/wiki/Hamaya "Hamaya")* ([破魔矢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A0%B4%E9%AD%94%E7%9F%A2 "wiktionary:破魔矢"), lit. 'evil-banishing arrow')** – Decorative arrows bought for good luck at Shinto shrines at New Year's and kept at home all year.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Hama Yumi](/wiki/Hama_Yumi "Hama Yumi")* ([破魔弓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A0%B4%E9%AD%94%E5%BC%93 "wiktionary:破魔弓"), lit. 'evil-banishing bow')** – A sacred bow.
* ***Han-[honji suijaku](/wiki/Honji_suijaku "Honji suijaku")* ([本地垂迹](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AC%E5%9C%B0%E5%9E%82%E8%BF%B9 "wiktionary:本地垂迹"))** – A theory conceived by Yoshida Kanetomo which reversed the standard *honji suijaku* theory, asserting Buddhist gods were [avatars](/wiki/Avatars "Avatars") of Japanese *kami*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Hanagasa* ([花笠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%8A%B1%E7%AC%A0 "wiktionary:花笠"))** – A flowered-hat worn by *miko* during festivals.
* ***[Hannya](/wiki/Hannya "Hannya")* ([般若](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%88%AC%E8%8B%A5 "wiktionary:般若"), lit. '[Prajñā](/wiki/Praj%C3%B1%C4%81_(Buddhism) "Prajñā (Buddhism)")')** – A female *yōkai* found in Japanese folklore (and in *[kagura](/wiki/Kagura "Kagura")* and [Noh](/wiki/Noh_Theater "Noh Theater")), and is most often described as a monstrous *oni* of a female; a *hannya* is a *yōkai* who was originally once a normal mortal human woman, but one who has become so overcome with her jealousy that it has metaphorically consumed her, followed by literally transforming her.
* ***[Harae](/wiki/Harae "Harae")* ([祓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%93 "wiktionary:祓"), lit. 'purification')** – General term for rituals of purification in Shinto.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) Methods of purification include *[misogi](/wiki/Misogi "Misogi")*.
* ***Haraedo* ([祓戸](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%93%E6%88%B8 "wiktionary:祓戸"), lit. 'place of purification')** – A place where ritual purification is performed.
* ***Haraedo-no-Kami* ([祓戸の神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%93%E6%88%B8%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:祓戸の神"))** – *Kami* of purification. Amongst the many *kami* born when Izanagi performed *misogi* in order to cleanse the netherworld filth on him after he had returned from his futile attempt to retrieve his late consort, Izanami.
* ***[Haraegushi](/wiki/Haraegushi "Haraegushi")* ([祓串](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%93%E4%B8%B2 "wiktionary:祓串"), lit. 'purification wand')** – an *ōnusa* having a hexagonal or octagonal wand.
* ***Hassoku-an* ([八足案](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E8%B6%B3%E6%A1%88 "wiktionary:八足案"), lit. 'eight-legged table')** – See *an*.
* ***[Hatsumōde](/wiki/Hatsum%C5%8Dde "Hatsumōde")* ([初詣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%9D%E8%A9%A3 "wiktionary:初詣"), lit. 'first accomplishment')** – The first shrine visit of the [New Year](/wiki/Japanese_New_Year "Japanese New Year").
* ***[Hatsuhinode](/wiki/First_sunrise "First sunrise")* ([初日の出](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%9D%E6%97%A5%E3%81%AE%E5%87%BA "wiktionary:初日の出"), lit. 'First Rising of the Sun')** – The first sunrise of the New Year.
* ***[Hatsuyume](/wiki/Hatsuyume "Hatsuyume")* ([初夢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%9D%E5%A4%A2 "wiktionary:初夢"), lit. 'first dream')** – The first dream that a person has in the New Year. In Shinto, it is believed that the subjects of the first dreams of the year are representative of what one's upcoming year will be like.
* ***[Heiden](/wiki/Heiden_(Shinto) "Heiden (Shinto)")* ([火産霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%81%AB%E7%94%A3%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:火産霊"), lit. 'fireborn spirit')** – A section of a shrine where offerings are presented to the gods.
* ***Heihaku* ([幣帛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%A3%E5%B8%9B "wiktionary:幣帛"))** – See also *go-hei*.
* ***Heishi* ([瓶子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%93%B6%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:瓶子"), lit. 'vase')** – Small bottles used for holding offerings, such as *sake*; numbered amongst the *shingu* for holding offerings.
* ***[Himorogi](/wiki/Himorogi "Himorogi")* ([神籬](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%B1%AC "wiktionary:神籬"), lit. 'divine fence')** – Temporary sacred spaces or altars used to worship. *Himorogi* are usually areas demarcated with green bamboo or [sakaki](/wiki/Sakaki "Sakaki") at the four corners supporting *[shimenawa](/wiki/Shimenawa "Shimenawa")*.
* ***[Hinamatsuri](/wiki/Hinamatsuri "Hinamatsuri")* ([雛祭り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9B%9B%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:雛祭り"), lit. 'girls' festival')** – A ceremony held on 3 March, celebrating the women of Japan, as well as expressing wishes for their continued good health. Originally celebrated as the "Peach Festival", it became known as *Hina-Matsuri* during the reign of [Empress Meishō](/wiki/Empress_Meish%C5%8D "Empress Meishō").
* ***Hi-no-tama* ([火の玉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%81%AB%E3%81%AE%E7%8E%89 "wiktionary:火の玉"), lit. 'balls of fire')** – Fireballs whose presence indicate supernatural activity.
* ***Hiōgi* ([檜扇](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%AA%9C%E6%89%87 "wiktionary:檜扇"), lit. 'cypress fan')** – A fan used originally by Heian aristocrats, and today by Shinto priests in formal settings.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) See also *akomeôgi* (above)
* ***[Hirairi](/wiki/Hirairi "Hirairi")* ([平入](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%B3%E5%85%A5 "wiktionary:平入"))** – A style of construction in which the building has its main entrance on the side that runs parallel to the roof's ridge (non gabled-side). The *[shinmei-zukuri](/wiki/Shinmei-zukuri "Shinmei-zukuri")*, *[nagare-zukuri](/wiki/Nagare-zukuri "Nagare-zukuri")*, *[hachiman-zukuri](/wiki/Hachiman-zukuri "Hachiman-zukuri")*, and *[hie-zukuri](/wiki/Hie-zukuri "Hie-zukuri")* styles belong to this type.
* ***Hirazara* ([平皿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%B3%E7%9A%BF "wiktionary:平皿"), lit. 'flat plate')** – Numbered amongst the *shingu* (tools used in shrine altars and home altars) for holding offerings, specifically one for holding [rice](/wiki/Rice "Rice") and one for holding [salt](/wiki/Salt "Salt").
* ***[Hitobashira](/wiki/Hitobashira "Hitobashira")* ([人柱](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BA%BA%E6%9F%B1 "wiktionary:人柱"), lit. 'human pillar')** – A human sacrifice, buried alive under or near large-scale buildings like dams, bridges and castles. *Hitobashira* can also refer to workers who were buried alive under inhumane conditions.
* ***[Hitodama](/wiki/Hitodama "Hitodama")* ([人魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BA%BA%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:人魂"), lit. 'human soul')** – The wayward soul of a human being who has already died.
* ***[Hitorigami](/wiki/Hitorigami "Hitorigami")*** ([独神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%AC%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:独神"), lit. 'monad *kami*') – Shinto *kami* who came into being alone, as opposed to those who came into being as male-female pairs.
* ***Hōhei/hōbei* ([奉幣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A5%89%E5%B9%A3 "wiktionary:奉幣"), lit. 'money offering')** – Offerings made to a *kami*, usually consisting in *heihaku*, but sometimes of jewels, money, weapons or other objects.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Hokosaki Suzu* ([鉾先鈴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%89%BE%E5%85%88%E9%88%B4 "wiktionary:鉾先鈴"), lit. 'Halberd Bell')** – Similar to a *Kagura Suzu*, a *Hokosaki Suzu* is a set of several bells on a short-staff.
* ***[Hōko](/wiki/H%C5%8Dko_(doll) "Hōko (doll)")* ([這子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%80%99%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:這子"), lit. 'crawling child')** – A soft-bodied doll given to young women of age and pregnant women in Japan as an amulet to protect both the new mother and the unborn child.
* ***[Hokora/hokuraman](/wiki/Hokora "Hokora")* ([祠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%A0 "wiktionary:祠") or [神庫](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%BA%AB "wiktionary:神庫"))** – An extremely small shrine. One of the earliest words for *shrine*.
* ***[Ho-musubi](/wiki/Kagutsuchi "Kagutsuchi")* ([火産霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%81%AB%E7%94%A3%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:火産霊"))** – Another name for Kagutsuchi.
* ***[Honden](/wiki/Honden "Honden")* ([本殿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AC%E6%AE%BF "wiktionary:本殿"), lit. 'main hall')** – Also called *shinden* (神殿) ("divine hall"), the *honden* is the most sacred building of a shrine, intended for the exclusive use of the enshrined *kami*.
* ***[Honji suijaku](/wiki/Honji_suijaku "Honji suijaku")* ([本地垂迹](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AC%E5%9C%B0%E5%9E%82%E8%BF%B9 "wiktionary:本地垂迹"))** – A theory dominant for centuries in Japan according to which Japanese *kami* are local manifestations of Indian gods.
* ***Hongū* ([本宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AC%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:本宮"), lit. 'main shrine')** – Located only within a *jingū*, the main shrine enshrining the principal *kami*, as opposed to *betsugū*, *sessha* or *massha*. The term includes *haiden*, *heiden* and *honden*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) See also *honsha*.
* ***Honsha* ([本社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:本社"))** – The main shrine of a shrine complex. It is followed hierarchically by *sessha* and *massha*.
* ***[Hōsōshi](/wiki/Fangxiangshi#Japanese_hōsōshi "Fangxiangshi")* ([方相氏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%96%B9%E7%9B%B8%E6%B0%8F "wiktionary:方相氏"))** – An obsolete term for the role driving away devils at a religious ceremony, or the driver of the hearse carrying the coffin of a deceased emperor, back in ancient Japan; a [ritual](/wiki/Ritual "Ritual") [exorcist](/wiki/Exorcist "Exorcist") fulfilling a role in a funeral, called *tsuina* (see below). Originally a [Tang dynasty](/wiki/Tang_dynasty "Tang dynasty") Chinese custom, later adopted by the Japanese during the Heian period.
* ***Hotoke* ([仏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%8F "wiktionary:仏"), lit. 'Buddha')** – A term meaning either *Buddha* or *dead soul*. While Buddhist in origin, the term is used in the second sense by all Japanese religions.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Hyakudoishi* ([百度石](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%99%BE%E5%BA%A6%E7%9F%B3 "wiktionary:百度石"), lit. 'hundred-times stone')** – Sometimes present as a point of reference for the *hyakudomairi* near the entrance of a shrine or Buddhist temple.
* ***Hyakudomairi* ([百度参り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%99%BE%E5%BA%A6%E5%8F%82%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:百度参り"), lit. 'hundred-times visits')** – A worshipper with a special prayer will visit the shrine a hundred times. After praying, they must go at least back to the entrance or around a *hyakudoishi* for the next visit to be counted as separate.
* ***[Hyōi](/wiki/Spirit_possession "Spirit possession")* ([憑依](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%86%91%E4%BE%9D "wiktionary:憑依"), lit. 'Possession')** – Possession, specifically possession by a spirit or a *kami*.
