## I

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: I")]

* ***Ichijama* ([生邪魔](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%9F%E9%82%AA%E9%AD%94 "wiktionary:生邪魔"), lit. 'living evil spirit')** – A type of curse from [Okinawa](/wiki/Okinawa_Prefecture "Okinawa Prefecture"); it is a type of *ikiryō*—-a spirit of a still-living person which leaves the body to haunt its victim. An *ichijama* is enacted using a special doll known as an *ichijama butokii*. See also *Ushi no Koku Mairi*.
* ***Ichijama Butokii* ([生邪魔仏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%9F%E9%82%AA%E9%AD%94%E4%BB%8F "wiktionary:生邪魔仏"), lit. 'living evil spirit image')** – A doll used to enact an *ichijama* curse.
* ***Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto* ([市杵島姫命](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B8%82%E6%9D%B5%E5%B3%B6%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD "wiktionary:市杵島姫命"))** – See Benzaiten.
* ***Ichirei Shikon* ([一霊四魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%80%E9%9C%8A%E5%9B%9B%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:一霊四魂"), lit. 'one spirit, four souls')** – A [philosophy](/wiki/Philosophy "Philosophy") within Shinto in which one's soul consists of a whole spirit called *naohi* ([直霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9B%B4%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:直霊")) that is connected with the heaven and the *shikon*: the *ara-mitama*, *kushi-mitama*, *nigi-mitama*, and *saki-mitama*.
* ***[Ihai](/wiki/Spirit_tablet "Spirit tablet")* ([位牌](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BD%8D%E7%89%8C "wiktionary:位牌"))** – A [placard](/wiki/Placard "Placard") used to designate the seat of a deity or past ancestor. The name of the deity or past ancestor is usually inscribed onto the tablet. With origins in traditional Chinese culture, the spirit tablet is a common sight in many East Asian countries where any form of ancestor veneration is practiced.
* ***Ikan* ([衣冠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A1%A3%E5%86%A0 "wiktionary:衣冠"))** – A set of official robes worn by aristocrats and court officials of the Heian-era court. Worn today in Shinto by a *kannushi* in formal costume for festivals.
* ***[Ikiryō](/wiki/Ikiry%C5%8D "Ikiryō")* ([生霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%9F%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:生霊"))** – In Japanese popular belief, [folklore](/wiki/Folklore "Folklore") and fiction, it refers to a disembodied spirit that leaves the body of a person who is still living and subsequently haunts other people or places, sometimes across great distances..
* ***Imi* ([忌み](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%8C%E3%81%BF "wiktionary:忌み"), lit. 'abhorrent')** – Something to be avoided, particularly to a ceremony.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) See also *kegare* and *tsumi*.
* ***Imikotoba* ([忌み言葉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%8C%E3%81%BF%E8%A8%80%E8%91%89 "wiktionary:忌み言葉"), lit. 'abhorrent words')** – Words to be avoided in certain occasions.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Inari Ōkami](/wiki/Inari_%C5%8Ckami "Inari Ōkami")\* ([稲荷大神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:稲荷大神"))** – The Shinto *kami* of fertility, rice, agriculture, foxes, industry, and worldly success. Inari's shrines can be identified by the stone foxes which adorn it.
* ***Ireisai* ([慰霊祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%85%B0%E9%9C%8A%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:慰霊祭"), lit. 'consoling spirits festival')** – A festival to remember and pacify the spirits of war dead that takes place at [Yasukuni Shrine](/wiki/Yasukuni_Shrine "Yasukuni Shrine") and other shrines built to the purpose.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine")** ([伊勢神宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:伊勢神宮"), lit. 'The Great Forces Divine Palace') – A shrine in [Mie prefecture](/wiki/Mie_prefecture "Mie prefecture") considered one of the holiest *Shinto* sites.
* ***[Itako](/wiki/Itako "Itako")/Ichiko* ([神巫](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%B7%AB "wiktionary:神巫"), [巫子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B7%AB%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:巫子") & [市子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B8%82%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:市子"))** – The blind female [shamans](/wiki/Shamans "Shamans") from northwest Honshu who act as a link between human beings and *kami*, echoing what was probably the former role of *miko* in Shinto.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Iwakura](/wiki/Yorishiro#Iwakura "Yorishiro")\** ([磐座](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A3%90%E5%BA%A7 "wiktionary:磐座"))** – A rock where a *kami* has been invited to descend for worship. See *[yorishiro](/wiki/Yorishiro "Yorishiro")*.
* **[Izanagi](/wiki/Izanagi "Izanagi") ([イザナギ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%82%A4%E3%82%B6%E3%83%8A%E3%82%AE "wiktionary:イザナギ"))** – The [brother-husband](/wiki/Sibling_relationship#marriage "Sibling relationship") of Izanami, Izanagi is one of the Japanese creator *kami*; according to the *Nihongi* and *Kojiki*, he gave birth to Japan,[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) and is the father of Amaterasu, Tsukuyomi and Susanoo.
* **[Izanami](/wiki/Izanami "Izanami") ([イザナミ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%82%A4%E3%82%B6%E3%83%8A%E3%83%9F "wiktionary:イザナミ"))** – The [sister-wife](/wiki/Sister-wife "Sister-wife") of Izanagi. She is one of the Japanese creator *kami*, according to the *Nihongi* and *Kojiki*, gave birth to Japan,[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) later dying in childbirth with her last child, Kagutsuchi, who burned her alive and sent her to the Underworld, Izanami becomes a *kami* of death.

## J

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: J")]

* ***[Jaki](/wiki/Amanojaku "Amanojaku")* ([邪鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%82%AA%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:邪鬼"), lit. 'malevolent spirits')** – An *oni*-like creature in Japanese folklore, thought to be able to provoke a person's darkest desires. Similar to the *amanojaku*.
* ***Jama* ([邪魔](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%82%AA%E9%AD%94 "wiktionary:邪魔"), lit. 'malevolent demons')** – A demon or devil of perversity, a hindrance to the practice of purity in Shinto and the practice of enlightenment in Buddhism.
* ***Jichinsai* ([地鎮祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9C%B0%E9%8E%AE%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:地鎮祭"), lit. 'ground-pacifying ceremony')** – A ceremony held by a Shinto priest on a site before the start of construction on the behalf of owners and workers to pacify and appease local spirits.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Jidai Matsuri](/wiki/Jidai_Matsuri "Jidai Matsuri")* ([時代祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:時代祭"), lit. the "festival of the ages")** – One of the three main annual festivals held in Kyoto, Japan (the other two being the Aoi Matsuri and the Gion Festival). It is held on October 22 every year.
* ***[Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism) "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:地獄"), lit. 'earth prison')** – The Shinto-Buddhist version of [Hell](/wiki/Hell "Hell") (the Japanese name for *[Diyu](/wiki/Diyu "Diyu")*). Similar to the [Nine Rings of Hell](/wiki/Dante%27s_Inferno#Nine_circles_of_Hell "Dante's Inferno") in *[Dante's Inferno](/wiki/Dante%27s_Inferno "Dante's Inferno")*, *Jigoku* has eight levels.
* ***[Jikininki](/wiki/Jikininki "Jikininki")* ([食人鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A3%9F%E4%BA%BA%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:食人鬼"), lit. 'human-eating ghost')** – In Japanese Buddhism, *jikininki* are the spirits of greedy, selfish, or impious individuals who are cursed after death to seek out and eat human corpses. See also *gaki*.
* ***[Jin](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([仁](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%81 "wiktionary:仁"), lit. 'benevolence')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Jingikan](/wiki/Jingikan "Jingikan")* ([神祇官](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%87%E5%AE%98 "wiktionary:神祇官"), lit. 'Divine Land Spirits Governmental Office')** – In the *[ritsuryō](/wiki/Ritsury%C5%8D "Ritsuryō")* system, the part of government responsible for festivals.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Jingū* ([神宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:神宮"), lit. 'Divine Palace/Shrine')** – A shrine enshrining a member of the Imperial family, like [Meiji Jingū](/wiki/Meiji_Shrine "Meiji Shrine"), which enshrines the deified spirits of [Emperor Meiji](/wiki/Emperor_Meiji "Emperor Meiji") and [Empress Shōken](/wiki/Empress_Sh%C5%8Dken "Empress Shōken").
* ***[Jingū-ji](/wiki/Jing%C5%AB-ji "Jingū-ji")* ([神宮寺](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%AF%BA "wiktionary:神宮寺"))** – A temple whose existence is supposed to help the soul of the *kami* the shrine next to it enshrines.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Jinja](/wiki/Shinto_shrine "Shinto shrine")\** ([神社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:神社"))** – The most general name for a shrine.
* ***Jinja-bukkaku* ([神社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:神社"))** – Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples, especially a combined shrine/temple complex.
* ***Jinja-fukkyū* ([神社復旧](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%BE%A9%E6%97%A7 "wiktionary:神社復旧"))** – A reversal of the [Meiji period](/wiki/Meiji_period "Meiji period")'s *jinja gappei*. Not to be confused with *jinja fukushi*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Jinja-fukushi* ([神社福祉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E7%A6%8F%E7%A5%89 "wiktionary:神社福祉"))** – A form of unofficial and illegal restoration of a merged shrine. See *jinja-gappei*.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Jinja-gappei* ([神社合併](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%90%88%E4%BD%B5 "wiktionary:神社合併"))** – A policy begun in the early 1900s, when half the shrines were merged with the remainder and disappeared.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Jinja-honchō](/wiki/Jinja_Honch%C5%8D "Jinja Honchō")* ([神社本庁](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E6%9C%AC%E5%BA%81 "wiktionary:神社本庁"))** – Also known as the Association of Shinto Shrines, it is a group that includes most of the Shinto shrines in Japan.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Jinja-kaikan* ([神社会館](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BC%9A%E9%A4%A8 "wiktionary:神社会館"))** – A hotel-like building inside large shrines used for weddings.
* ***Jinja-shinto* ([神社神道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:神社神道"))** – Originally a synonym of State Shinto (Kokka Shinto below), it is now a term criticized by specialists as problematic.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) When applied to post-war Shinto, it means the beliefs and practices associated to shrines, particularly those associated with the Association of Shinto Shrines.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Jisei](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([自制](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%87%AA%E5%88%B6 "wiktionary:自制"), lit. '[self-restraint](/wiki/Self-restraint "Self-restraint")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Jisha](/wiki/Chinjusha "Chinjusha")* ([寺社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AF%BA%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:寺社"))** – A temple's tutelary shrine. See also *chinjusha* (above) and *jinja-bukkaku*.
* ***[Jōe](/wiki/J%C5%8De "Jōe")* ([浄衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B5%84%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:浄衣"), lit. 'pure robes')** – A formal over-garment worn by *kannushi* during religious ceremonies; a silk *kariginu*.
* ***Jōtō* (上棟, "Raising the Roof")** – A Shinto ceremony held when raising the main beam that forms the ridge of the roof.
* ***Junpai* ([巡拝](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B7%A1%E6%8B%9D "wiktionary:巡拝"), lit. 'patrol route')** – The custom of visiting a fixed series of 33 or 88 shrines or temples, or shrines-&-temples.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Junrei](/wiki/Junrei "Junrei")* ([巡礼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B7%A1%E7%A4%BC "wiktionary:巡礼"), lit. 'patrol etiquette')** – The term most commonly used for either of two major types of [pilgrimages](/wiki/Pilgrimage "Pilgrimage") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), in accordance with Buddhism or Shinto. These pilgrimages can be made as a visit to a group of [temples](/wiki/Temple "Temple"), [shrines](/wiki/Jinja_(Shinto) "Jinja (Shinto)"), or other holy sites, in a particular order, often in a circuit of 33 or 88 sites.

## K

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: K")]

* ***[Kabura-ya](/wiki/Kabura-ya "Kabura-ya")* ([鏑矢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8F%91%E7%9F%A2 "wiktionary:鏑矢"), lit. 'turnip arrow')** – In Shinto, the sound made by the *kabura-ya* in mid-flight is thought to [ward-off evil influences](/wiki/Apotropaic_magic "Apotropaic magic"); like the *hamaya*, the *kabura-ya* is used in Shinto cleansing rites of sites, shrine grounds, and parks.
* ***Kabuto* ([冑](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%86%91 "wiktionary:冑") or [兜](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%9C "wiktionary:兜"), lit. 'helmet')** – A helmet—complete with a [suit of armour](/wiki/Japanese_armour "Japanese armour")—sometimes dedicated to shrines, and indicative of a *kami's* power to ward-off and protect from negative influences.
* ***[Kadomatsu](/wiki/Kadomatsu "Kadomatsu")* ([門松](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%96%80%E6%9D%BE "wiktionary:門松"), lit. 'pine gateposts')** – New Year decorations placed in pairs in front of homes to welcome the *kami* of the harvest.
* ***[Kagami](/wiki/Mirror "Mirror")* ([鏡](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8F%A1 "wiktionary:鏡"), lit. 'mirror')** – Often used in Shinto worship; originally [bronze mirrors](/wiki/Bronze_mirror#China "Bronze mirror") were used (see also *shinkyō*), having been introduced to Japan from China; the most famous example of mirrors in *Shinto* is the *[Yata no Kagami](/wiki/Yata_no_Kagami "Yata no Kagami")*.
* ***[Kagami](/wiki/Kagutsuchi "Kagutsuchi")* ([火神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%81%AB%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:火神"), lit. 'fire deity')** – Another name for Kagatsuchi.
* **[Kagome crest](/wiki/Kagome_crest "Kagome crest")** ([籠目紋](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B1%A0%E7%9B%AE%E7%B4%8B "wiktionary:籠目紋"), lit. 'lattice eye crest') – An [apotropaic mark](/wiki/Apotropaic_mark "Apotropaic mark"), specifically [hexagrams](/wiki/Hexagram "Hexagram") and [octagrams](/wiki/Octagram "Octagram") featured in Shinto shrines, including the [Ise Grand Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Grand_Shrine "Ise Grand Shrine"), to ward off negative and malevolent influences and evil spirits.
* ***[Kagura](/wiki/Kagura "Kagura")* ([神楽](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD "wiktionary:神楽"), lit. 'divine entertainment')** – A type of Shinto dance with deep ties to the [Emperor](/wiki/Emperor "Emperor") and his family, accompanied by instruments, which is also called *mikagura* (御神楽). It is also a type of Shinto dance performed at shrines during religious rites, and is also called *satokagura* (里神楽).
* ***[Kagura-den](/wiki/Kagura-den "Kagura-den")* ([神楽殿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%E6%AE%BF "wiktionary:神楽殿"), lit. 'divine entertainment platform')** – a pavilion or stage dedicated to the performing of the *kagura*. Also called *maidono* or *buden* (舞殿).
* ***[Kagura suzu](/wiki/Kagura_suzu "Kagura suzu")* ([神楽鈴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%E9%88%B4 "wiktionary:神楽鈴"), lit. 'divine entertainment bells')** – a set of twelve bells used in *kagura*.
* ***[Kagari-bi](/wiki/Fire_worship "Fire worship")* ([篝火](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AF%9D%E7%81%AB "wiktionary:篝火"))** – [Candle](/wiki/Candle "Candle") holders, also called *rōsoku-tate* ([ローソク立](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%83%AD%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BD%E3%82%AF%E7%AB%8B "wiktionary:ローソク立"), lit. 'Candle Holders'). They are designed for burning tiny white candles that are lit whenever one visits the *kamidana* for prayers. Some people use little electric lanterns instead of candles.
* ***[Kagutsuchi](/wiki/Kagutsuchi "Kagutsuchi")* ([迦具土](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%BF%A6%E5%85%B7%E5%9C%9F "wiktionary:迦具土"))** – The Shinto fire god and patron deity of blacksmiths and ceramic workers.
* ***Kajishin* ([鍛冶神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8D%9B%E5%86%B6%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:鍛冶神"), lit. 'forge deity')** – A divinity of the [blacksmith](/wiki/Blacksmith "Blacksmith")'s [forge](/wiki/Forge "Forge").
* ***Kakue* ([格衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A0%BC%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:格衣"))** – A traditional overcoat-robe worn by Shinto monks.
* ***Kakuremi* ([隠身](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9A%A0%E8%BA%AB "wiktionary:隠身"), lit. 'concealed form')** – A term for a hidden form, invisible form; metaphysical form (of a deity). Also known as *kakurimi*.
* ***Kakuriyo* ([隠り世](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9A%A0%E3%82%8A%E4%B8%96 "wiktionary:隠り世"), lit. 'hidden world')** – Refers to the worlds of *kami* and the dead.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* *[Kamado-gami](/wiki/Kamado-gami "Kamado-gami")* ([竃神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AB%83%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:竃神"), lit. 'kitchen deity') a *kami* which lives in people's ovens.
* ***Kamakura* ([かまくら](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8F%E3%82%89 "wiktionary:かまくら") or カマクラ, lit. 'warehouse')**:
  + A winter festival in Akita Prefecture, in mid-January/February, during which children get to play in quinzhee-like snow huts.
  + The [quinzhee](/wiki/Quinzhee "Quinzhee")-like [snow huts](/wiki/Snow_hut "Snow hut") made for children during the winter festival of the same-name, in mid-January/February, in Akita Prefecture.
* ***Kamakura Daimyojin* (鎌倉大明神, lit. 'Warehouse Great Light Kami')**: A Shinto *Kami* in Akita Prefecture revered during the *Kamakura* festival. See also *[Suijin](/wiki/Suijin "Suijin")*.
* ***[Kami](/wiki/Kami "Kami")* ([神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:神"), lit. 'Spirit, God, Deity, Divinity')** – A term broadly meaning *spirit* or *deity*, but has several separate meanings:
  + deities mentioned in Japanese mythologies and local deities protecting areas, villages and families.[[6]](#cite_note-smyers-6)
  + unnamed and non-anthropomorphic spirits found in natural phenomena.[[6]](#cite_note-smyers-6)
  + a general sense of sacred power.[[6]](#cite_note-smyers-6)
  + according to [Motoori Norinaga](/wiki/Motoori_Norinaga "Motoori Norinaga"), a *kami* is "any thing or phenomenon that produces the emotions of fear and awe, with no distinction between good and evil".[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* ***Kamiarizuki* ([神有月](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%9C%89%E6%9C%88 "wiktionary:神有月"))** – A [lunar calendar](/wiki/Lunar_calendar "Lunar calendar") month corresponding roughly to October. Because it is believed that in that month all *kami* go to [Izumo Taisha](/wiki/Izumo_Taisha "Izumo Taisha"), it is called "month with gods" in Izumo.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) See also *Kannazuki*.
* ***[Kamidana](/wiki/Kamidana "Kamidana")\** ([神棚](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%9A "wiktionary:神棚"), lit. 'Kami Shelf')** – A miniature shrine placed or hung high on a wall in some Japanese homes.
* ***[Kamikakushi](/wiki/Kamikakushi "Kamikakushi")*** ([神隠し](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%9A%A0%E3%81%97 "wiktionary:神隠し")) – A term used to refer to the mysterious disappearance or death of a person that happens when an angered god takes a person away. Japanese folklore contains numerous tales of humans abducted to the spirit world by *kami*. See also *tengu-kakushi*.
* ***[Kamikaze](/wiki/Kamikaze_(typhoon) "Kamikaze (typhoon)")* ([神風](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%A2%A8 "wiktionary:神風"), lit. 'divine wind')** – The two major typhoons that dispersed [Mongol-Koryo](/wiki/Goryeo_under_Mongol_rule "Goryeo under Mongol rule") fleets [who invaded Japan](/wiki/Mongol_invasions_of_Japan "Mongol invasions of Japan") under [Kublai Khan](/wiki/Kublai_Khan "Kublai Khan") in 1274.
* ***Kamimukae* ([神迎え](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E8%BF%8E%E3%81%88 "wiktionary:神迎え"))** – The first part of a typical festival.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1) The spirit is usually invited to a *himorogi*.
* ***[Kanai Anzen](/wiki/Kanai_Anzen "Kanai Anzen")* ([家内安全](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%B6%E5%86%85%E5%AE%89%E5%85%A8 "wiktionary:家内安全"))** – A specific kind of *omamori* meant to safeguard the safety of one's family.
* ***Kanayago* ([金屋子神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%87%91%E5%B1%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:金屋子神"))** – a god of blacksmiths, mainly in Chugoku Region (Shintoism).
* ***[Kanjō](/wiki/Kanj%C5%8D "Kanjō")* ([勧請](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8B%A7%E8%AB%8B "wiktionary:勧請"))** – A process through which a *kami* is transferred to a new shrine. See also *bunrei*.
* ***[Kanju](/wiki/Tide_jewels "Tide jewels")* ([干珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%B2%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:干珠"))** – One of two magical gems that Ryūjin used to control the tides; its counterpart is the *Manju*.
* ***Kansan* ([汗衫](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B1%97%E8%A1%AB "wiktionary:汗衫"))** – A thin jacket for girls of the aristocracy of the Heian period. Now worn by *miko* in formal attire for ceremonies and festivals.
* ***Kanmuri* ([冠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%86%A0 "wiktionary:冠"))** – A formal traditional [headdress](/wiki/Headdress "Headdress") worn by the Japanese emperor and by aristocratic men of the Heian period when formally dressed. Today, it in worn in Shinto by a *kannushi* in formal costume for formal ceremonies and festivals.
* ***[Kannagara](/wiki/Kannagara "Kannagara")* ([惟神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%83%9F%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:惟神"))** – Another word for Shinto.
* ***Kannagara no Michi* ([惟神の道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%83%9F%E7%A5%9E%E3%81%AE%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:惟神の道"), lit. the "Way of the Kami")** – Another name for Shinto used before World War II.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Kannazuki* ([神無月](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%84%A1%E6%9C%88 "wiktionary:神無月"))** – See also Kamiarizuki.
* ***[Kannushi](/wiki/Kannushi "Kannushi")\** ([神主](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%B8%BB "wiktionary:神主"))** – A Shinto priest who is a master of shrine ceremonies, rituals, and festivals.
* ***[Kariginu](/wiki/Kariginu "Kariginu")* ([狩衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%A9%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:狩衣"), lit. 'hunting robes')** – A style of cloak, originally the costume that Heian-era nobles wore when they went out hunting, which became the nobles' daily casual clothes. Worn today in Shinto by a *kannushi* in formal costume for rituals, ceremonies and festivals.
* ***[Kasoegi](/wiki/Kasoegi "Kasoegi")* ([斗木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%96%97%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:斗木"))** – See also *katsuogi*.
* ***Kasuga* ([春日](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5 "wiktionary:春日"), lit. 'springtime sun')** – The tutelary *kami* (*[ujigami](/wiki/Ujigami "Ujigami")*) of the entire [Yamato province](/wiki/Yamato_province "Yamato province").[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Kasuga-zukuri](/wiki/Kasuga-zukuri "Kasuga-zukuri")* ([春日造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:春日造"))** – The architectural style of [Kasuga-taisha](/wiki/Kasuga-taisha "Kasuga-taisha").
* ***[Katana](/wiki/Katana "Katana")* ([刀](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%80 "wiktionary:刀"), lit. 'blade')** – A [sword](/wiki/Sword "Sword"), sometimes dedicated to shrines (along with other weapons, such as *naginata* and spears), and indicative of a *kami's* power to ward off negative influences.
* ***Katashiro* ([形代](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BD%A2%E4%BB%A3 "wiktionary:形代"), model substitute)** – A traditional Japanese doll, made of materials like paper or straw, used in certain purification rituals, used as a substitute for a person, as the target for a prayer or curse cast against them.
* ***[Katsuogi](/wiki/Katsuogi "Katsuogi")\** ([鰹木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%B0%B9%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:鰹木"), [勝男木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8B%9D%E7%94%B7%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:勝男木"), [葛緒木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%91%9B%E7%B7%92%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:葛緒木"))** – A style of short decorative poles on a shrine's roof set at a right angle to the roof's ridgepole. See also *kasoegi*.
* ***[Kawaya-no-Kami](/wiki/Toilet_god "Toilet god")* ([厠の神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8E%A0%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:厠の神"), lit. '[latrine](/wiki/Latrine "Latrine") deity')** – The *kami* of latrines, toilets, [waterclosets](/wiki/Water-closet "Water-closet"), and feces.
* ***Kazaori Eboshi* ([風折烏帽子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A2%A8%E6%8A%98%E7%83%8F%E5%B8%BD%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:風折烏帽子"))** – A type of [pointed hat](/wiki/Pointed_hat "Pointed hat") originally worn by Heian-era aristocrats and samurai. It is now worn by *kannushi* as formalwear for occasions such as festivals and weddings.
* ***[Kekkai](/w/index.php?title=Kekkai_(Buddhism)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kekkai (Buddhism) (page does not exist)")* ([結界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B5%90%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:結界"), lit. 'Fastened-off World')** – a restriction in access to a specific area, barred-off often for the purposes of training &/or purification in esoteric Buddhism (and Hinduism).
* ***Kenkai* ([見界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A6%8B%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:見界"), lit. 'visible world')** – The world that one can see without any kind of supernatural gift; the world of the living.
* ***Kenkon* ([乾坤](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B9%BE%E5%9D%A4 "wiktionary:乾坤"))** – A term used to refer to the union of opposites.
* ***Kenzokushin* ([眷属神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9C%B7%E5%B1%9E%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:眷属神"), lit. 'household ancillary/domestic deities')** – A type of lesser *kami* or *yōkai* who serves a higher-ranked *kami* or *yōkai*.
* ***[Ki](/wiki/Qi "Qi")* ([氣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%A3 "wiktionary:氣") or [気](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%97 "wiktionary:気"))** – A vital force forming part of any living entity. See also *haku*.
* ***[Kijo](/wiki/Kijo_(folklore) "Kijo (folklore)")* ([鬼女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:鬼女"))** – An *oni* woman from Japanese legends. See also *onibabā* (below) and *yama-uba* (below).
* ***[Kimon](/wiki/Oni#Demon_gate "Oni")* ([鬼門](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E9%96%80 "wiktionary:鬼門"))** – Based on the [assignment of the twelve zodiac animals to the cardinal directions](/wiki/Earthly_Branches#Directions "Earthly Branches"), the direction of northeast, known as the direction of *ushi-tora* ([丑寅](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%91%E5%AF%85 "wiktionary:丑寅"), lit. 'Ox-Tiger'), is also-known-as the direction of *Kimon*; one theory is that the *oni's* cowlike horns and tigerskin loincloth developed as a visual depiction of this term. According to Taoism and esoteric *onmyōdō*, the northeasterly direction is considered an unlucky direction through which evil spirits pass, and, as-such, is termed as *Kimon*; having to travel in this direction was seen as a bad omen for a journey.
* ***[Kegare](/wiki/Kegare "Kegare")* ([穢れ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A9%A2%E3%82%8C "wiktionary:穢れ"), lit. 'impurity')** – defilement due to natural phenomena, for example the contact with dead bodies. The opposite of *kegare* is *kiyomi*. See also *imi* (above) and *tsumi* (below).
* ***[Kibitsu-zukuri](/wiki/Kibitsu-zukuri "Kibitsu-zukuri")* ([吉備津造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%B4%A5%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:吉備津造"))** – The architectural style of [Kibitsu Shrine](/wiki/Kibitsu_Shrine_(Bitch%C5%AB) "Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)") in [Okayama prefecture](/wiki/Okayama_prefecture "Okayama prefecture"), characterized by a huge *honden* divided in three parts with an interior painted in vermilion, black and gold.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Kigen* ([寄絃](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AF%84%E7%B5%83 "wiktionary:寄絃"))** – A ritual (i.e. during *shihobarai*) of banishing evil spirits and other negative influences, which can be achieved by the strumming of bows, such as a *hama yumi* and an *azusa yumi*.
* **[Kirin](/wiki/Qilin "Qilin") ([麒麟](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BA%92%E9%BA%9F "wiktionary:麒麟"))** – A mythical creature in Asian mythology; in Shinto, the kirin are considered messengers of the *kami*.
* ***[Kisshōten](/wiki/Kissh%C5%8Dten "Kisshōten")* ([吉祥天](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%90%89%E7%A5%A5%E5%A4%A9 "wiktionary:吉祥天"), lit. 'Auspicious Heavens')** – A Japanese goddess of good fortune, [wealth](/wiki/Wealth "Wealth") and [prosperity](/wiki/Prosperity "Prosperity"). Adapted, via Buddhism, from the Hindu goddess [Lakshmi](/wiki/Lakshmi "Lakshmi"). Kisshōten is sometimes named as one of the Seven Gods of Fortune, replacing either Jurōjin or Fukurokuju.
* ***[Kitsune](/wiki/Kitsune "Kitsune")\** ([狐](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%90 "wiktionary:狐"), lit. 'fox')** – Animal believed to have magical powers and to be a messenger to Inari. Inari shrines are always protected by statues of foxes, sometimes wearing red votive bibs.
* ***[Kitsunebi](/wiki/Kitsunebi "Kitsunebi")* ([狐火](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%90%E7%81%AB "wiktionary:狐火"), lit. 'fox fire')** – The [atmospheric ghost lights](/wiki/Atmospheric_ghost_lights "Atmospheric ghost lights") mentioned in legends all across Japan outside [Okinawa Prefecture](/wiki/Okinawa_Prefecture "Okinawa Prefecture").
* ***[Kitsune no yomeiri](/wiki/Kitsune_no_yomeiri "Kitsune no yomeiri")* ([狐の嫁入り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%90%E3%81%AE%E5%AB%81%E5%85%A5%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:狐の嫁入り"), lit. 'fox wedding')** – The *kitsune no yomeiri* can refer to several things: [atmospheric ghost lights](/wiki/Atmospheric_ghost_light "Atmospheric ghost light"); a sunshower; and various strange wedding processions that can be seen in classical Japanese *[kaidan](/wiki/Kaidan "Kaidan")*, essays, and legends. The *kitsune-no-yomeiri* is always closely related to foxes.
* ***Kiyome* ([清](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B8%85 "wiktionary:清"), lit. 'purity')** – The concept of purity within Shinto, the state in which all beings start-out as at birth, and can return to again by undergoing acts of *harae*, such as *misogi*. The opposite of *kiyomi* is *kegare*.
* ***[Kō](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_''Bushidō''_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([孝](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AD%9D "wiktionary:孝"), lit. '[Filial piety](/wiki/Filial_piety "Filial piety")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***Kō* ([講](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%AC%9B "wiktionary:講"), lit. 'lecture')** – A lay worship group focusing on a particular *kami* or sacred location which may perform pilgrimages and other rites.
* ***[Koban](/wiki/Koban_(coin) "Koban (coin)")* ([小判](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B0%8F%E5%88%A4 "wiktionary:小判"), lit. 'small ingot')** – Originally currency in the Edo period. In modern times, they are sold as *engimono* from Shinto shrines, or, at-least, symbolically, gold-foil cardboard versions are given in the place of the solid-gold ingots, particularly as decorations for a *kumade*, both attained from Shinto shrines for good fortune in business.
* ***Kōdō* ([皇道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9A%87%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:皇道"), lit. the "Imperial Way")** – Shinto as defined by post-Meiji nationalist.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* **[Kogakkan University](/wiki/Kogakkan_University "Kogakkan University") ([皇学館大学](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%A6%E9%A4%A8%E5%A4%A7%E5%AD%A6 "wiktionary:皇学館大学"))** – A university located near Ise Shrine that is one of two universities authorized to train Shinto priests.
* ***[Kojiki](/wiki/Kojiki "Kojiki")* ([古事記](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8F%A4%E4%BA%8B%E8%A8%98 "wiktionary:古事記"), lit. 'Records of Ancient Matters' or 'An Account of Ancient Matters')** – An early Japanese written chronicle of myths, legends, songs, genealogies, oral traditions, and semi-historical accounts down to 711–712 AD. Similar to the *Nihon Shoki*.
* ***Kokka Shinto* ([国家神道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9B%BD%E5%AE%B6%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:国家神道"), lit. 'State Shinto')** – Japanese translation of the English term *State Shinto* created in 1945 by the US occupation forces to define the post-Meiji religious system in Japan.
* ***Kokoro* ([心](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%83 "wiktionary:心"), lit. 'heart')** – The essence of a thing or being.
* ***[Kokugakuin Daigaku](/wiki/Kokugakuin_University "Kokugakuin University")* ([國學院大學](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9C%8B%E5%AD%B8%E9%99%A2%E5%A4%A7%E5%AD%B8 "wiktionary:國學院大學"))** – Tokyo university that is one of two authorized to train Shinto priests.
* ***[Kokujō Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([黒縄地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BB%92%E7%B8%84%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:黒縄地獄"), lit. 'hell of black rope')** – The second level of *Jigoku*, where sinners, who have committed murder and theft are.
* ***[Komainu](/wiki/Komainu "Komainu")\** ([狛犬](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%9B%E7%8A%AC "wiktionary:狛犬"))** – Stone warden [maned](/wiki/Mane_(lion) "Mane (lion)")-dogs usually present at the entrance of a shrine.
* ***[Kon](/wiki/Hun_and_po "Hun and po")* ([魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:魂"), lit. 'soul')** – Stemming from Taoism, *kon* is the part of the soul that goes to heaven and is able to leave the body, carrying with it an appearance of physical form; the subliminal self. See also *tamashii*.
* ***Konaoshi* ([小直衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%B4%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:小直衣"))** – Attire of the *Heian* court, now worn by *kannushi* in formal functions.
* ***[Kongōsho](/wiki/Vajra "Vajra")* ([金剛杵](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%87%91%E5%89%9B%E6%9D%B5 "wiktionary:金剛杵"))** – A ritual weapon, resembling a *[yawara](/wiki/Yawara "Yawara")* and a *[vajra-mushti](/wiki/Vajra-mushti "Vajra-mushti")*.
* ***[Konjin](/wiki/Konjin "Konjin")* ([金神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%87%91%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:金神"))** – An itinerant *kami* from *onmyōdō*. Konjin is associated with compass directions, and said to change position with the year, lunar month, and season.
* ***Kono-yo* ([この世](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%93%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%96 "wiktionary:この世"), lit. 'this world')** – See *busshitsukai*.
* ***[Konpaku](/wiki/Hun_and_po "Hun and po")* ([魂魄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%82%E9%AD%84 "wiktionary:魂魄"))** – Every living human has both a *kon* and a *haku*.
* ***[Kome](/wiki/Rice#Cultural_roles_of_rice "Rice")* ([米](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B1%B3 "wiktionary:米"), lit. 'Rice')** – Offerings of white rice made at Shinto shrines and a household's *kamidana*. See also *ō-kome*.
* ***[Kotoamatsukami](/wiki/Kotoamatsukami "Kotoamatsukami")* ([別天神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%A5%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:別天神"))** – The collective name for the first *Kami* which came into existence at the time of the creation of the universe, according to the *Kokiji*. They were came into being up in Takamagahara at the time of creation. Unlike later gods, these deities were born without any procreation. See also *Amatsukami* (above).
* ***Kuchiyose miko* ([口寄巫女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8F%A3%E5%AF%84%E5%B7%AB%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:口寄巫女"), lit. 'channelling miko')** – A *miko* acting as a [spirit medium](/wiki/Mediumship "Mediumship").
* ***[Kuebiko](/wiki/Kuebiko "Kuebiko")* ([久延毘古](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B9%85%E5%BB%B6%E6%AF%98%E5%8F%A4 "wiktionary:久延毘古"))** – A Shinto *kami* of local knowledge and agriculture, represented in Japanese mythology as a [scarecrow](/wiki/Scarecrow "Scarecrow"), who cannot walk but has comprehensive self-awareness and [omniscience](/wiki/Omniscience "Omniscience").
* ***[Kuji-in](/wiki/Kuji-in#Meanings_in_Shinto "Kuji-in")* ([九字印](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B9%9D%E5%AD%97%E5%8D%B0 "wiktionary:九字印"), lit. 'Nine Hand Seals')** – A system of *[mudras](/wiki/Mudras "Mudras")* and associated *[mantras](/wiki/Mantras "Mantras")* that consist of nine syllables.
* ***[Kuji-kiri](/wiki/Kuji-kiri "Kuji-kiri")* ([九字切り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B9%9D%E5%AD%97%E5%88%87%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:九字切り"), lit. 'Nine Symbolic Cuts')** – A system of *mudras* and associated *mantras* that consist of nine syllables, based on *Kuji-in*.
* ***Kumade* ([熊手](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%86%8A%E6%89%8B "wiktionary:熊手"), lit. 'bear hand')** – A [rake](/wiki/Rake_(tool)#Cultural_associations "Rake (tool)"); a smaller, handheld, decorated version is sold as an *engimono* and is believed to be able to, literally, rake-in good-fortune &/or rake-out bad-fortune for the user.
* ***[Kumo](/wiki/Cloud "Cloud")* ([雲](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9B%B2 "wiktionary:雲"), lit. 'cloud')** – Synonymous with heaven; in the event that a household *kamidana* cannot be installed in the highest point of the house, the *Kanji* for 'Cloud' is written on a piece of paper and affixed above the *kamidana*; doing this lets the *kami* know that, while they should be enshrined at the highest point, circumstances prevent this from being-so.
* ***[Kunitokotachi-no-mikoto](/wiki/Kunitokotachi "Kunitokotachi")* ([国之常立神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9B%BD%E4%B9%8B%E5%B8%B8%E7%AB%8B%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:国之常立神"))** – A *kami* considered to be the most important by [Yoshida Kanetomo](/wiki/Yoshida_Kanetomo "Yoshida Kanetomo") and considered important also by Watarai Shinto.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***Kunitsu tsumi* ([国津罪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9B%BD%E6%B4%A5%E7%BD%AA "wiktionary:国津罪")/[国つ罪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9B%BD%E3%81%A4%E7%BD%AA "wiktionary:国つ罪"))** – A term for *tsumi* specifically committed on Earth. The corresponding concept is *amatsu tsumi*.
* ***[Kushi-mitama](/wiki/Mitama "Mitama")* ([奇魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A5%87%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:奇魂"))** – The wise and experienced side of a *mitama*. The *kushi-mitama* is associated with the colours blue and green, and with the cardinal direction of east.
* ***[Kusudama](/wiki/Kusudama "Kusudama")* ([薬玉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%96%AC%E7%8E%89 "wiktionary:薬玉"), lit. 'medicine ball')** – Originating from ancient Japanese culture, *kusudama* were used for [incense](/wiki/Incense "Incense") and [potpourri](/wiki/Potpourri "Potpourri"); possibly originally being actual bunches of flowers or herbs. They are now typically used as decorations or as gifts.
* ***[Kuwabara kuwabara](/wiki/Kuwabara_kuwabara "Kuwabara kuwabara")* ([桑原桑原](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A1%91%E5%8E%9F%E6%A1%91%E5%8E%9F "wiktionary:桑原桑原"), lit. '[mulberry](/wiki/Mulberry "Mulberry") field')** – A phrase used in the Japanese language to ward off lightning. It is analogous to the English phrase "knock on wood" to prevent bad luck.
* ***Kyōha Shinto* ([教派神道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%95%99%E6%B4%BE%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:教派神道"), lit. 'sect Shinto')** – A label applied to certain sects by the Meiji government to give them an official status.[[1]](#cite_note-bocking-1)
* ***[Kyōkan Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([叫喚地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8F%AB%E5%96%9A%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:叫喚地獄"), lit. 'Hell of Screaming')** – The fourth level of *Jigoku*, where sinners, who have committed murder, theft, degeneration, and drunkenness are sent.

## M

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: M")]

* ***[Madai](/wiki/Pagrus_major "Pagrus major")* ([真鯛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9C%9F%E9%AF%9B "wiktionary:真鯛"), lit. 'genuine seabream')** – A fish native to Japan. It is also featured among *Engimono* (above), as its red colour is seen as [auspicious](/wiki/Color_in_Chinese_culture#Red_/_Vermilion "Color in Chinese culture"). The *madai* is often seen with [Ebisu](/wiki/Ebisu_(mythology) "Ebisu (mythology)"), as he is the patron *kami* of fisherman and one of the Seven Lucky Gods.
* ***[Magatama](/wiki/Magatama "Magatama")\** ([勾玉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8B%BE%E7%8E%89 "wiktionary:勾玉") or [曲玉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9B%B2%E7%8E%89 "wiktionary:曲玉"), lit. 'curved jewel')** – A comma-shaped jewel, often used as a *yorishiro*. See also *Yasakani no Magatama*.
* ***[Makoto](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([誠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%AA%A0 "wiktionary:誠"), lit. 'honesty')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Manju](/wiki/Tide_jewels "Tide jewels")* ([満珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%BA%80%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:満珠"))** – One of two magical gems that Ryūjin used to control the tides; its counterpart is the *Kanju*.
* ***[Maneki-neko](/wiki/Maneki-neko "Maneki-neko")* ([招き猫](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%8B%9B%E3%81%8D%E7%8C%AB "wiktionary:招き猫"), lit. 'beckoning cat')** – A protective and good luck talisman.
* ***[Marebito](/wiki/Marebito "Marebito")* ([稀人](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A8%80%E4%BA%BA "wiktionary:稀人") or [客人](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%A2%E4%BA%BA "wiktionary:客人"))** – A concept of a spiritual or divine being that visits from the 'other world' (takai, a term for the Japanese afterlife) at specific times and, thus, must be shown the height of [hospitality](/wiki/Hospitality "Hospitality").
* ***[Massha](/wiki/Setsumatsusha "Setsumatsusha")\** ([末社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%AB%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:末社"), lit. 'descendant shrine')** – A synonym of *sessha*.
* ***Mayoke* ([魔除け](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%94%E9%99%A4%E3%81%91 "wiktionary:魔除け"))** – A ward against evil.
* ***Meido* ([冥土](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%86%A5%E5%9C%9F "wiktionary:冥土"))** – Another name for Yomi.
* ***Meikai* ([冥界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%86%A5%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:冥界"), lit. 'dark world')** – Another word for Yomi.
* ***[Meiyo](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([名誉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%90%8D%E8%AA%89 "wiktionary:名誉"), lit. '[Honour](/wiki/Honour "Honour")')** – One of the seven-plus [Virtues](/wiki/Virtues "Virtues") of *[Bushido](/wiki/Bushido "Bushido")*.
* ***[Miko](/wiki/Miko "Miko")\** ([巫女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B7%AB%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:巫女"), [神子](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AD%90 "wiktionary:神子"), [巫](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B7%AB "wiktionary:巫") or [覡](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A6%A1 "wiktionary:覡"))** – A woman who helps *kannushi* in their work, or a woman possessing magic powers and capable of giving fortunes.
* ***[Mikoshi](/wiki/Mikoshi "Mikoshi")* ([神輿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E8%BC%BF "wiktionary:神輿"))** – A divine palanquin.
* ***[Misogi](/wiki/Misogi "Misogi")\** ([禊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A6%8A "wiktionary:禊"))** – An ascetic practice of ritual ablution purification.
* ***[Mitama](/wiki/Mitama "Mitama")* ([御魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:御魂"), [御霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:御霊") or [神霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:神霊"), lit. 'honourable soul')** – The spirit of a *kami* or the soul of a dead person.[[7]](#cite_note-kojien-7) The opposite of *mitama* is *tamashii*. See also *ara-mitama*, *kushi-mitama*, *nigi-mitama* and *saki-mitama*.
* ***Miya* ([宮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%AE "wiktionary:宮"), lit. 'palace')** – A term that often defines a shrine enshrining a special *kami* or a member of the Imperial household, for example an empress. See also *Ō-miya* (below)
* ***[Miyamairi](/wiki/Miyamairi "Miyamairi")* ([宮参り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%AE%E5%8F%82%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:宮参り"), lit. 'shrine visit')** – A traditional rite of passage for newborns held at a shrine.
* ***Mizutama* ([水玉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%B4%E7%8E%89 "wiktionary:水玉"), lit. 'water orb')** – A small, droplet-shaped vessel, used for holding offerings of fresh water on a *kamidama*, and is to be changed daily; belongs to the *shingu* for holding offerings.
* ***[Mochitsuki](/wiki/Mochi#Seasonal_specialties "Mochi")* ([餅搗き](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A4%85%E6%90%97%E3%81%8D "wiktionary:餅搗き"), もちつき)** – A traditional activity for occasions like Japanese New-Year, rice is pounded into *[mochi](/wiki/Mochi "Mochi")* and is eaten in hopes of gaining good fortune over the coming year. The activity is associated with the moon, jade, or golden rabbit, which according to East-Asian folklore, is said to pound rice (or the [elixir of life](/wiki/Elixir_of_life "Elixir of life")) in its [mortar and pestle](/wiki/Mortar_and_pestle "Mortar and pestle"), at the behest of the *kami*.
* ***[Momijigari](/wiki/Leaf_peeping#Japan "Leaf peeping")* ([紅葉狩](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B4%85%E8%91%89%E7%8B%A9 "wiktionary:紅葉狩"), lit. 'red leaf hunting')** – The Japanese traditional festival and custom of enjoying the transient beauty of leaves changing colour in the autumn; the Japanese tradition of going to visit scenic areas where leaves have turned red in the autumn; particularly [maple](/wiki/Maple "Maple") tree leaves.
* ***[Mononoke](/wiki/Mononoke "Mononoke")* ([物の怪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%89%A9%E3%81%AE%E6%80%AA "wiktionary:物の怪"))** – A [monstrous](/wiki/Monster "Monster") apparition; a monster.
* ***Mori* ([杜](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9D%9C "wiktionary:杜") or [森](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A3%AE "wiktionary:森"), lit. 'forest')** – A wood, a forest, a grove, specifically a grove or forest on shrine grounds. It reflects close relationship between trees and shrines. Tree worship is common in Shinto.
* ***Muenbotoke* ([無縁仏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%84%A1%E7%B8%81%E4%BB%8F "wiktionary:無縁仏"))** – The soul or spirit of a deceased human with no living connections amongst the living; the dead who have no living relatives); similarly to *gaki* and *jikininki*, a *muenbotoke* can be appeased by a *sagaki*.
* ***[Mugen Jigoku](/wiki/Av%C4%ABci "Avīci")* ([無間地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%84%A1%E9%96%93%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:無間地獄"))** – The eighth and deepest level of *Jigoku*, where sinners that have committed murder, theft, degeneration, drunkenness, lying, blasphemy, and rape, [parricide](/wiki/Parricide "Parricide"), and assassination of holy men are sent.
* ***Musubi-no-Kami* ([結びの神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B5%90%E3%81%B3%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:結びの神"), lit. 'deity of binding')** – One of the Shinto *kami* of creation; also known as the *kami* of matchmaking, love, and marriages.
* ***Musuhi* ([産霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%A3%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:産霊"))** – A term in Shinto for the spiritual influences that produces all the things in the universe and helps them develop and complete their cycle.
* ***Myōjin* ([明神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:明神"), lit. 'bright deity')** – A term that refers to a title, historically applied to *kami* and their shrines. See also *sannō*.

## N

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: N")]

* ***[Nagashi-bina](/wiki/Hinamatsuri#Customs "Hinamatsuri")* ([流し雛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B5%81%E3%81%97%E9%9B%9B "wiktionary:流し雛"))** – Ritual purification ceremonies held where participants make dolls out of materials such as paper or straw and send them on a boat down a river, carrying one's impurities and sin with them.
* ***[Naginata](/wiki/Naginata "Naginata")* ([薙刀](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%96%99%E5%88%80 "wiktionary:薙刀"), lit. 'weeding blade')** – A [polearm](/wiki/Polearm "Polearm"), sometimes dedicated to shrines (along with other weapons, such as *katana* and spears), and indicative of a *kami's* power to ward off negative influences.
* ***[Namahage](/wiki/Namahage "Namahage")* ([生剥](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%9F%E5%89%A5 "wiktionary:生剥"), lit. 'raw peel')** – In traditional [Japanese folklore](/wiki/Japanese_folklore "Japanese folklore") is a demon-like being, portrayed by men wearing hefty *oni* masks and traditional straw capes during a New Year's ritual.
* ***Nanakusa-gayū* ([七草粥](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%83%E8%8D%89%E7%B2%A5 "wiktionary:七草粥"))** – A traditional meal of congee and herbs served on *Nanakusa-no-Sekku*. The seven herbs are [Japanese parsley](/wiki/Oenanthe_javanica "Oenanthe javanica"), [shepherd's purse](/wiki/Shepherd%27s_purse "Shepherd's purse"), *[Pseudognaphalium affine](/wiki/Pseudognaphalium_affine "Pseudognaphalium affine")*, [chickweed](/wiki/Chickweed "Chickweed"), [nipplewort](/wiki/Nipplewort "Nipplewort"), turnips, and [daikon](/wiki/Daikon "Daikon").
* ***[Nanakusa-no-Sekku](/wiki/Nanakusa-no-sekku "Nanakusa-no-sekku")* ([七草の節句](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%83%E8%8D%89%E3%81%AE%E7%AF%80%E5%8F%A5 "wiktionary:七草の節句"), lit. 'seven-herb festival')** – One of the five annual traditional festivals held throughout the year. It is observed on 7 January.
* ***Nanareizan* ([七霊山](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%83%E9%9C%8A%E5%B1%B1 "wiktionary:七霊山"), lit. 'seven spiritual mountains')** – Seven mountains revered as sacred in Shinto and Buddhism; they are: Mount Fuji, Mount Haku, Mount Tate, [Mount Ōmine](/wiki/Mount_%C5%8Cmine "Mount Ōmine"), [Mount Shakka](/wiki/Mount_Shakka "Mount Shakka"), [Mount Daisen](/wiki/Mount_Daisen "Mount Daisen"), and [Mount Ishizuchi](/wiki/Mount_Ishizuchi "Mount Ishizuchi").
* ***[Naraku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism) "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([奈落](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A5%88%E8%90%BD "wiktionary:奈落"))** – The Hell realm of incarnation, the lowest and worst realm on the wheel of reincarnation. See *rinne*.
* ***[Nenju](/wiki/Japamala "Japamala")* ([念珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%B5%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:念珠") lit. 'thought beads')** – Shinto-Buddhist [rosary](/wiki/Rosary "Rosary"); a string or necklace of beads used for prayers.
* ***[Ne-no-kuni](/wiki/Ne-no-kuni "Ne-no-kuni")* ([根の国](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A0%B9%E3%81%AE%E5%9B%BD "wiktionary:根の国"), lit. 'land of roots')** – A term referring to a netherworld or limbo in Japanese mythology, like the Sanzu River. It is sometimes considered to be identical to Yomi, but darker, as well as *Tokoyo-no-kuni*.
* ***[Nigi-mitama](/wiki/Mitama "Mitama")* ([和魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%92%8C%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:和魂"), lit. 'tranquil soul')** – The friendly and cooperative side of a complete *mitama*. It is associated with the colour white and the cardinal direction of west.
* ***[Nihon Shoki](/wiki/Nihon_Shoki "Nihon Shoki")* ([日本書紀](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E6%9B%B8%E7%B4%80 "wiktionary:日本書紀"))** – An early Japanese written chronicle of myths, legends, songs, genealogies, oral traditions, and semi-historical accounts down to 720 AD. Similar to the *Kojiki*.
* ***[Ningen](/wiki/Human_beings_in_Buddhism "Human beings in Buddhism")* ([人間](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BA%BA%E9%96%93 "wiktionary:人間"))** – The human realm of incarnation, the third-highest realm on the wheel of reincarnation. See *Rinne* (below).
* ***[Nio](/wiki/Nio_(Buddhism) "Nio (Buddhism)")* ([仁王](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%81%E7%8E%8B "wiktionary:仁王"))** – In Shinto-Buddhism, *nio* is the Japanese name for the *Kongōrikishi*, the two wrathful and muscular guardians of the Buddha standing at the entrance of many Buddhist temples. See also *a-un*, and Gozu and Mezu.
* ***[Norito](/wiki/Norito "Norito")* ([祝詞](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9D%E8%A9%9E "wiktionary:祝詞"), lit. 'invocation scripts')** – [liturgical](/wiki/Liturgical "Liturgical") texts or ritual incantations in Shinto, usually addressed to a given *kami*.
* ***Norito* ([祝](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9D "wiktionary:祝"), lit. 'blessing')** – Written spells for blessings.
* ***Noroi* ([呪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%91%AA "wiktionary:呪"), [詛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A9%9B "wiktionary:詛") & [咒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%92%92 "wiktionary:咒"), lit. 'curse')** – Written spells for curses.
* ***[Noshi](/wiki/Noshi "Noshi")* ([熨斗](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%86%A8%E6%96%97 "wiktionary:熨斗"))** – Ceremonial pieces of paper wrapped in a sheet of coloured paper folded in a long hexagonal shape that are attached to gifts and presents offered on festive occasions in Japan.
* ***[Nusa](/wiki/%C5%8Cnusa "Ōnusa")* ([幣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%A3 "wiktionary:幣"))** – See *ōnusa*; a wooden wand used in Shinto rituals. It is decorated with many *shide*.
* ***[Nyoihōju](/wiki/Cintamani "Cintamani")* ([如意宝珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%82%E6%84%8F%E5%AE%9D%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:如意宝珠"), lit. 'As-One-Wishes Jewel')** – A wish-fulfilling jewel within both [Hindu](/wiki/Hindu "Hindu") and [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhist "Buddhist") traditions, and the Eastern equivalent of the [philosopher's stone](/wiki/Philosopher%27s_stone "Philosopher's stone") in Western alchemy. It is one of several [Mani Jewel](/wiki/Mani_Jewel "Mani Jewel") images found in Buddhist scripture. The *Nyoihōju* is commonly-depicted within the hands of Kisshōten, as-well-as in the hands of a bodhisattva in art.

## O

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: O")]

* ***Obake* ([御化け](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E5%8C%96%E3%81%91 "wiktionary:御化け") or [お化け](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E5%8C%96%E3%81%91 "wiktionary:お化け"))** – An apparition or spectre.
* ***[Ofuda](/wiki/Ofuda "Ofuda")* ([御札](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%AD "wiktionary:御札") or [お札](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E6%9C%AD "wiktionary:お札"))** – Written paper spells, amulets and talismans. See also *omamori*.
* ***[Okame](/wiki/Hyottoko "Hyottoko")* ([阿亀](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%98%BF%E4%BA%80 "wiktionary:阿亀"))** – Also called Otafuku. It is a mask that depicts the face of a woman who has a short nose and swollen round cheeks.
* ***[O-kome](/wiki/Rice#Cultural_roles_of_rice "Rice")* ([御米](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E7%B1%B3 "wiktionary:御米"))** – Offerings of white rice made at Shinto shrines and a household's *kamidana*. See also *kome*.
* ***[O-kuizome](/wiki/Childbirth_in_Japan#Newborn "Childbirth in Japan")* ([お食い初め](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E9%A3%9F%E3%81%84%E5%88%9D%E3%82%81 "wiktionary:お食い初め"), lit. 'first food')** – When a baby is 100 days old, Japanese families celebrate a weaning ceremony called *O-Kuizome*. This ceremony traditionally involves a large shared meal prepared by the mother-in-law.
* **[Ōkuninushi](/wiki/%C5%8Ckuninushi "Ōkuninushi")** ([大国主神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E5%9B%BD%E4%B8%BB%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:大国主神"), lit. 'Master of the Great Land' or 'Great Master of the Land') – see Daikokuten.
* ***Ōmagatoki* ([逢魔時](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%80%A2%E9%AD%94%E6%99%82 "wiktionary:逢魔時") and [大禍時](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E7%A6%8D%E6%99%82 "wiktionary:大禍時"))** – A term referring to the moment at twilight, when the sky grows dark. *Ōmagatoki* is the time when *chimimōryō*, the evil spirits of the mountains and rivers, attempt to materialise in the World of the Living.
* ***[Omamori](/wiki/Omamori "Omamori")* ([お守り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E5%AE%88%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:お守り"))** – [amulets](/wiki/Amulet "Amulet") and [talismans](/wiki/Talisman "Talisman") available at shrines and temples for particular purposes, for example health or success in business.
* ***Omigoro* ([小忌衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B0%8F%E5%BF%8C%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:小忌衣"), lit. 'small mourning robes')** – An overcoat robe used for Shinto services.
* ***[Ō-miki](/wiki/Sake#Ceremonial_use "Sake")* ([御神酒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E7%A5%9E%E9%85%92 "wiktionary:御神酒") or [神酒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%85%92 "wiktionary:神酒"))** – Offerings of white rice wine made at Shinto shrines and a household's *kamidana*. It is often consumed as part of Shinto purification rituals. See also *sake*.
* ***[Omikuji](/wiki/Omikuji "Omikuji")\** ([御御籤](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E5%BE%A1%E7%B1%A4 "wiktionary:御御籤") or [御神籤](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%B1%A4 "wiktionary:御神籤"))** – fortunes written on strips of paper that are often found at shrines wrapped around tree branches.
* ***[Oni](/wiki/Oni "Oni")* ([鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:鬼"))** – In one interpretation of an *oni*, they are a kind of *yōkai*, whereas another interpretation of an *oni* is as something completely separate from a *yōkai* (although both are supernatural monsters). See also *jikininki* and *gaki*.
* ***[Onibabā](/wiki/Kijo_(folklore) "Kijo (folklore)")* ([鬼婆](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E5%A9%86 "wiktionary:鬼婆"))** – An *oni* woman from Japanese legends. See also *kijo* and *yama-uba*.
* ***[Onibi](/wiki/Onibi "Onibi")* ([鬼火](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E7%81%AB "wiktionary:鬼火"))** – A type of atmospheric ghost light in legends of Japan; according to folklore, they can be anything from fires caused by *oni*, to the spirits born from the corpses of humans and animals. They are also said to be resentful people that have become fire and appeared.
* ***[Onigawara](/wiki/Onigawara "Onigawara")* ([鬼瓦](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E7%93%A6 "wiktionary:鬼瓦"), lit. 'Oni Tile(s)')** – A type of roof ornamentation found in Japanese architecture. They are generally roof tiles or statues depicting an *oni*. Prior to the Heian period, similar ornaments with floral and plant designs (*hanagawara*) preceded the *onigawara*.
* ***Oni hitokuchi* ([鬼一口](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E4%B8%80%E5%8F%A3 "wiktionary:鬼一口"))** – A term similar to *kamikakushi* and *tengukakushi* (below), referring-to the mysterious disappearance or death of a person without warning or without a trace.
* ***[Oni-wa-Soto! Fuku-wa-Uchi!](/wiki/Setsubun "Setsubun")* ([鬼は外! 福は内!](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AF%E5%A4%96!_%E7%A6%8F%E3%81%AF%E5%86%85! "wiktionary:鬼は外! 福は内!"), lit. 'Demons Out! Luck In!')** – A mantra for driving-off malevolent spirits, demons or devils that cause ill fortune to allow good fortune to work unimpeded.
* ***[Onmyōdō](/wiki/Onmy%C5%8Dd%C5%8D "Onmyōdō")* ([陰陽道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%99%B0%E9%99%BD%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:陰陽道"), lit. 'yin-yang way')** – A traditional Japanese [esoteric cosmology](/wiki/Japanese_creation_myth "Japanese creation myth"); a mixture of natural science and occultism.
* **[Onmyōji](/wiki/Onmy%C5%8Dji "Onmyōji")** ([陰陽師](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%99%B0%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%AB "wiktionary:陰陽師"), lit. 'yin-yang practitioner') – A practitioner of *onmyōdō*.
* ***Onmyōryō* ([陰陽寮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%99%B0%E9%99%BD%E5%AF%AE "wiktionary:陰陽寮"))** – A governmental office of *onmyōdō* that was responsible for timekeeping and calendar-making. They also documented and analysed omens and fortunes.
* ***[Onryō](/wiki/Onry%C5%8D "Onryō")* ([怨霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%80%A8%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:怨霊"), lit. 'vengeful spirit')** – A type of [vengeful spirit](/wiki/Vengeful_ghost "Vengeful ghost"); a [poltergeist](/wiki/Poltergeist "Poltergeist").
* ***[Ō-Nenju](/wiki/Japamala "Japamala")* ([念珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BF%B5%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:念珠") or [数珠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%95%B0%E7%8F%A0 "wiktionary:数珠"))** – A string of prayer beads commonly used in Hinduism, Jainism, Sikhism, Buddhism and Shinto for the spiritual practice known in Sanskrit as *[japa](/wiki/Japa "Japa")*.
* ***[Ō-Nusa](/wiki/%C5%8Cnusa "Ōnusa")* ([大幣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E5%B9%A3 "wiktionary:大幣"))** – Wooden wands used in rituals. Decorated with many *shide*, they are waved left and right during ceremonies.
* ***[Osechi](/wiki/Osechi "Osechi")* ([御節](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%A1%E7%AF%80 "wiktionary:御節"), lit. 'season')** – Also known as *osechi-ryōri* (御節料理 or お節料理), an *osechi* is a traditional spread of Japanese New Year foods. The tradition started in the Heian period (794–1185). *Osechi* are recognizable by their *[jūbako](/wiki/J%C5%ABbako "Jūbako")* (重箱), which resemble *bentō* boxes. *Osechi* includes dishes like *ozōni*.
* ***[O-Shichiya](/wiki/Childbirth_in_Japan#Newborn "Childbirth in Japan")* ([お七夜](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%9C "wiktionary:お七夜"), lit. 'seventh night')** – When a baby turns seven days old, families officially name the baby. The mother, the father, and the grandparents are often involved in this process.
* ***[Otafuku](/wiki/Hyottoko "Hyottoko")* ([阿多福](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%98%BF%E5%A4%9A%E7%A6%8F "wiktionary:阿多福"))** – A mask that represents a smiling Japanese woman with large, rosy cheeks. *Otafuku* is also known as Ame-no-Uzume
* ***[Ō-yashiro](/wiki/Shinto_shrine "Shinto shrine")* ([大社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:大社"))** – See also *Taisha*.
* ***[Ozen](/wiki/Plinth "Plinth")* ([お膳](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E8%86%B3 "wiktionary:お膳"), lit. 'Great Tray')** – A traditional Japanese four-legged tray used to carry food offerings.
