## R

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: R")]

* **[Raijin](/wiki/Raijin "Raijin") ([雷神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:雷神"))** – The *kami* of [thunder](/wiki/Thunder "Thunder") and [lightning](/wiki/Lightning "Lightning") who is the brother of Fūjin; together, they are both said to be two of the many sons and other children of Izanagi and Izanami.
* ***Rakan* ([羅漢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BE%85%E6%BC%A2 "wiktionary:羅漢"))** – An *[Arhat](/wiki/Arhat "Arhat")*, a Buddhist saint.
* ***[Rei](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([礼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A4%BC "wiktionary:礼"), lit. 'etiquette')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***Rei* ([霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:霊"), lit. 'spirit')** – The soul or spirit.
* ***Reibai* ([霊媒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AA%92 "wiktionary:霊媒"))** – A person who can sense the presence of spirits and other supernatural/paranormal phenomena.
* ***Reikai* ([霊界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:霊界"), lit. 'spirit world')** – See *seishinkai*.
* ***Reikan* ([霊感](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E6%84%9F "wiktionary:霊感"), lit. 'spirit sense')** – The ability to sense the presence of spirits and other supernatural/paranormal phenomena.
* ***Reiki* ([霊鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:霊鬼"))** – The soul or spirit of someone or something dead, particularly malevolent.
* ***Reikon* ([霊魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:霊魂"))** – The soul or spirit of a person, usually someone who has just died.
* ***Reinōryoku* ([霊能力](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E8%83%BD%E5%8A%9B "wiktionary:霊能力"), lit. 'Spirit Sensing Prowess')** – The ability to sense the presence of spirits and other supernatural/paranormal phenomena; the term for psychics in Japan is *Reinōsha* ([霊能者](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E8%83%BD%E8%80%85 "wiktionary:霊能者"), lit. 'Spiritual Ability Person').
* ***Reizan* ([霊山](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%B1%B1 "wiktionary:霊山"), lit. 'Spirit Mountain')** – A holy mountain.
* ***[Rin](/wiki/Standing_bell "Standing bell")* ([鈴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%88%B4 "wiktionary:鈴"))** – A type of standing bell or resting bell.
* ***[Rinne](/wiki/Sa%E1%B9%83s%C4%81ra_(Buddhism) "Saṃsāra (Buddhism)")* ([輪廻](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%BC%AA%E5%BB%BB "wiktionary:輪廻"))** – The concept of rebirth; the beginning-less cycle of repeated birth from within six realms of reincarnation, mundane existence and dying again.
* ***[Rokuyō](/wiki/Japanese_calendar#Rokuyō "Japanese calendar")*** ([六曜](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AD%E6%9B%9C "wiktionary:六曜")), the six-day week of the *Shinto-[Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism")* [calendar](/wiki/Calendar "Calendar").
* ***[Ryōbu Shintō](/wiki/Ry%C5%8Dbu_Shint%C5%8D "Ryōbu Shintō")* ([両部神道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%A1%E9%83%A8%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:両部神道"))** – Also called *shingon Shintō*, in Japanese religion, the syncretic school that combined Shinto with the teachings of the *Shingon* sect of *Buddhism*. The school developed during the late Heian and Kamakura periods. The basis of the school's beliefs was the Japanese concept that *kami* were manifestations of Buddhist divinities.
* ***[Ryūjin](/wiki/Ry%C5%ABjin "Ryūjin")* ([龍神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BE%8D%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:龍神"), lit. 'Dragon God')** – The [tutelary deity](/wiki/Tutelary_deity "Tutelary deity") of the seas and oceans in [Shinto](/wiki/Shinto "Shinto") [mythology](/wiki/Mythology "Mythology").

## S

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: S")]

* ***Saidan* ([祭壇](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%AD%E5%A3%87 "wiktionary:祭壇"), lit. 'festival podium')** – An altar or a structure to make offerings to *kami*.
* ***Saikigū* ([祭器具](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%AD%E5%99%A8%E5%85%B7 "wiktionary:祭器具"))** – The utensils used in religious ceremonies, including the *sanbō*, *oshiki*, *hassoku-an*, and *takatsuki*.
* **[Sakaki](/wiki/Sakaki "Sakaki")\* ([榊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A6%8A "wiktionary:榊"))** – A type of flowering evergreen tree native to Japan that is sacred in Shinto. Cuttings of sakaki, called *tamagushi*, are often offered as offerings at shrines and in rituals. Cuttings are also displayed on either side of a *kamidana* as offerings.
* ***Sakaki-tate* ([榊立](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A6%8A%E7%AB%8B "wiktionary:榊立"), lit. 'Sakaki Stands')** – A pair of small, white vases, used for displaying cuttings of sakaki are displayed on either side of a *kamidana* as offerings and at rituals.
* ***[Sake](/wiki/Sake "Sake")* ([酒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%85%92 "wiktionary:酒"))** – An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting white rice.
* ***[Saki-mitama](/wiki/Mitama "Mitama")* ([幸魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%B8%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:幸魂"))** – The happy and loving side of a complete *mitama*. It is associated with the colours red and pink, and with the cardinal direction of south.
* ***Sanbō* ([三方](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%89%E6%96%B9 "wiktionary:三方"), lit. 'three directions')** – A stand used to bear food offerings, usually made of unpainted [Japanese cypress](/wiki/Hinoki "Hinoki").
* ***[Saisen](/wiki/Saisen "Saisen")* ([賽銭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%B3%BD%E9%8A%AD "wiktionary:賽銭"))** – Offerings of money given by worshipers.
* **Saishi** ([祭祀](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%AD%E7%A5%80#Japanese "wiktionary:祭祀")) - rituals.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)
* ***Sanbon Jime* ([三本締め](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%89%E6%9C%AC%E7%B7%A0%E3%82%81 "wiktionary:三本締め"))** – A custom in Japan; rhythmic-clapping hands—and shouting-loudly—when celebrating. This is done when people are celebrating the successful end of an event.
* ***[Sandō](/wiki/Sand%C5%8D "Sandō")\** ([参道](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8F%82%E9%81%93 "wiktionary:参道"))** – The path leading from a *torii* to a shrine. The term is also used sometimes at Buddhist temples too.
* ***Sannō* ([山王](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%B1%E7%8E%8B "wiktionary:山王"), lit. 'Mountain King')** – A term that refers to, both a title for a type of *kami*, and a specific divine spirit that protects a divine mountain.
* ***Sanpai sahō* ([参拝作法](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8F%82%E6%8B%9D%E4%BD%9C%E6%B3%95 "wiktionary:参拝作法"))** – The way in which the Japanese worship at shrines, bowing twice, clapping twice, then bowing one last time.
* ***[Sanreizan](/wiki/Three_Holy_Mountains "Three Holy Mountains")* ([三霊山](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%89%E9%9C%8A%E5%B1%B1 "wiktionary:三霊山"), lit. 'three spiritual mountains')** – Three mountains revered as sacred in Shinto: Mount Fuji, Mount Haku, and Mount Tate. They are included amongst the *Nanareizan*.
* **[Sanzu River](/wiki/Sanzu_River "Sanzu River")** ([三途の川](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%89%E9%80%94%E3%81%AE%E5%B7%9D "wiktionary:三途の川"), lit. 'River of Three Crossings') – A mythological river that acts as a boundary between the lands of the living and the dead.
* ***Sei* ([精](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B2%BE "wiktionary:精"), lit. 'vitality')** – The energy of living things.
* ***Seichi* ([聖地](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%81%96%E5%9C%B0 "wiktionary:聖地"), lit. '[hallowed](/wiki/Hallow "Hallow") ground')** – Another word for *jōdo*.
* ***[Seijin-no-Hi](/wiki/Coming_of_Age_Day "Coming of Age Day")* ([成人の日](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%88%90%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5 "wiktionary:成人の日"))** – A Japanese holiday held annually on the second Monday of January. It is held to congratulate and encourage all those who have reached or will reach the age of maturity between 2 April of the previous year and 1 April of the current year.
* ***[Seimei Kikyō](/wiki/Platycodon#Japan "Platycodon")* ([晴明紋](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%99%B4%E6%98%8E%E7%B4%8B "wiktionary:晴明紋")/[晴明桔梗](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%99%B4%E6%98%8E%E6%A1%94%E6%A2%97 "wiktionary:晴明桔梗"))** – A pentacle that is the personal seal of [Abe-no-Seimei](/wiki/Abe_no_Seimei "Abe no Seimei"), later becoming the symbol for the *Onmyōryō* (the government ministry department for the practice of *onmyōdō*, or the Bureau of Taoist Geomancy) and for *onmyōdō* itself, given its association with the Five Elements.
* ***Seirei* ([精霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B2%BE%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:精霊"), lit. 'Soul')** – A general umbrella term for the essence, spirit or soul of a nonspecific thing.
* ***Seisatsu\** ([制札](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%88%B6%E6%9C%AD "wiktionary:制札"))** – A signboard containing announcements and rules for worshipers.
* ***[Seishinkai](/wiki/Spirit_world_(Spiritualism) "Spirit world (Spiritualism)")* ([精神界](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B2%BE%E7%A5%9E%E7%95%8C "wiktionary:精神界"), lit. 'spirit world')** – A spiritual, non-corporeal world that coexists with the material world, that human beings inhabit, but in a different dimension.
* ***[Senjafuda](/wiki/Senjafuda "Senjafuda")* ([千社札](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%83%E7%A4%BE%E6%9C%AD "wiktionary:千社札"), lit. 'thousand shrine tags')** – [Votive](/wiki/Votive "Votive") slips, stickers or placards (and *ofuda*) posted on the gates or buildings of Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples in Japan. The stickers bear the name of the worshipper, and can be purchased pre-printed with common names at temples and shrines throughout Japan, as well as at stationery stores and video game centres. *Senjafuda* were originally made from wooden slats, but have been made of paper since the [Edo period](/wiki/Edo_period "Edo period").
* ***[Sessha](/wiki/Setsumatsusha "Setsumatsusha")\** ([摂社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%91%82%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:摂社"))** – A smaller shrine housing a *kami* having a strong relationship with that of the *honsha* (the main shrine). A synonym of *massha*.
* ***[Setsubun](/wiki/Setsubun "Setsubun")* ([節分](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AF%80%E5%88%86 "wiktionary:節分"), lit. 'seasonal divide')** – A ceremony held on February 3 that celebrates the beginning of spring in Japan.
* ***[Shaku](/wiki/Shaku_(ritual_baton) "Shaku (ritual baton)")* ([笏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AC%8F "wiktionary:笏"))** – A flat baton often seen in portraits of noblemen and samurai, but also used by *kannushi*.
* ***Shamusho* ([社務所](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A4%BE%E5%8B%99%E6%89%80 "wiktionary:社務所"), lit. 'Shrine Business Establishment')** – A shrine's administrative office. It often sells *omamori* and other goods.
* ***[Shichi-Go-San](/wiki/Shichi-Go-San "Shichi-Go-San")* ([七五三](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%83%E4%BA%94%E4%B8%89 "wiktionary:七五三"), lit. 'seven-five-three')** – A traditional [rite of passage](/wiki/Rite_of_passage "Rite of passage") and festival day in Japan for three- and seven-year-old girls and five-year-old boys, held annually on November 15.
* ***[Shide](/wiki/Shide_(Shinto) "Shide (Shinto)")\** ([垂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9E%82 "wiktionary:垂"), [紙垂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B4%99%E5%9E%82 "wiktionary:紙垂") and [四手](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%8B "wiktionary:四手"))** – A zigzag-shaped paper streamer often attached to a *shimenawa* and to *tamagushi*, and is used in rituals.
* ***Shigai* ([絲鞋](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%B5%B2%E9%9E%8B "wiktionary:絲鞋"))** – [Moccasin](/wiki/Moccasin "Moccasin")-like footwear, made from silk with leather soles. Originally worn by children and young woman of the aristocratic-class, it is now (or, at-least was) worn by *miko* in Shinto rituals.
* ***[Shika](/wiki/Deer "Deer")* ([鹿](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%B9%BF "wiktionary:鹿"))** – A deer. In Shinto, they are considered messengers of the *kami*.
* ***[Shikome](/wiki/Shikome "Shikome")* ([黄泉醜女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BB%84%E6%B3%89%E9%86%9C%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:黄泉醜女"))** – Hags sent by Izanami to pursue Izanagi for shaming her. Their numbers differ between the *[Kojiki](/wiki/Kojiki "Kojiki")* and the *[Nihon Shoki](/wiki/Nihon_Shoki "Nihon Shoki")*.
* ***Shinbai* ([神媒](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AA%92 "wiktionary:神媒"))** – A divine spiritualist or medium.
* ***Shinbatsu* ([神罰](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%BD%B0 "wiktionary:神罰"), lit. 'divine punishment')** – Punishment dealt-out by a *kami* against transgressions committed by mortals and immortals alike.
* ***Shinboku* ([神木](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E6%9C%A8 "wiktionary:神木"), lit. 'divine tree')** – A tree considered divine.
* ***Shimekazari* ([注連飾り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B3%A8%E9%80%A3%E9%A3%BE%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:注連飾り"))** – A [wreath](/wiki/Wreath "Wreath") made of braided-*shimenawa* rope with auspicious additions interwoven into it. *Shimekazari* are put up as decorations for [Japanese New Year](/wiki/Japanese_New_Year "Japanese New Year"), along with *[kadomatsu](/wiki/Kadomatsu "Kadomatsu")*.
* ***[Shimenawa](/wiki/Shimenawa "Shimenawa")* ([標縄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A8%99%E7%B8%84 "wiktionary:標縄") / [注連縄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B3%A8%E9%80%A3%E7%B8%84 "wiktionary:注連縄") / [七五三縄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%83%E4%BA%94%E4%B8%89%E7%B8%84 "wiktionary:七五三縄"), lit. 'Enclosing Rope')** – Lengths of rope made from laid/woven/braided rice straw or hemp that are used for ritual purification.
* ***Shinen* ([神苑](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E8%8B%91 "wiktionary:神苑"), lit. 'divine gardens')** – The gardens on shrine grounds.
* ***Shingu* ([神具](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%85%B7 "wiktionary:神具"), lit. 'divine tool')** – A term for the items displayed upon a *kamidana* (see above).
* ***Shinidama* ([死魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%AD%BB%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:死魂"), lit. 'dead soul')** – The soul of a dead person.
* ***Shiniki* ([神域](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%9F%9F "wiktionary:神域"), lit. 'divine domain')** – The perimeters of a shrine or a place where *kami* dwell. It is also used to refer to any place of significant importance.
* ***Shinji* ([神事](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%BA%8B "wiktionary:神事"), lit. 'divine service')** – Rituals and services in Shinto.
* ***[Shinkan](/wiki/Shinkan_(official) "Shinkan (official)")* ([神官](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%98 "wiktionary:神官"), lit. 'divine official')** – A person serving a certain god or working as a government official at a facility where God is worshipped. *Shinkan* is also used as a term designating a Shinto priest (a person involved in religious services and office work in a shrine).
* ***Shingaku* ([神額](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%A1%8D "wiktionary:神額"))** – A plaque mounted to the front of the *torii* or main hall of a shrine, bearing the name of the shrine.
* ***Shinko* ([神狐](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E7%8B%90 "wiktionary:神狐"), lit. 'divine fox')** – Similar to *komainu*, *shinko* are twin dual statues of foxes, usually depicted as having white fur, who serve Inari Ōkami as messengers.
* ***Shinkyō* ([神鏡](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%8F%A1 "wiktionary:神鏡"), lit. 'divine mirror')** – A mirror that is often included in shrine altars and home altars; they are believed to represent the *kami* themselves.
* ***Shinra Banshō* ([森羅万象](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A3%AE%E7%BE%85%E4%B8%87%E8%B1%A1 "wiktionary:森羅万象"))** – A Shinto-Buddhist term, meaning "all things existing in the universe": According to Shinto, the *kami* exist within *shinra banshō*.
* ***Shinsōsai* ([神葬祭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E8%91%AC%E7%A5%AD "wiktionary:神葬祭"))** – A Shinto funeral service.
* ***Shinza* ([神座](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%BA%A7 "wiktionary:神座"), lit. 'divine seat')** – A place where there is a *kami* or a place where housing the sacred object of a shrine, like a *[sanctum sanctorum](/wiki/Sanctum_sanctorum "Sanctum sanctorum")*.
* ***[Shinzen-Kekkonshiki](/wiki/Shinto_wedding "Shinto wedding")* ([神前結婚式](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E5%89%8D%E7%B5%90%E5%A9%9A%E5%BC%8F "wiktionary:神前結婚式"))** – A Shinto wedding ceremony.
* ***[Shio](/wiki/Salt#In_religion "Salt")* ([塩](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A1%A9 "wiktionary:塩"), lit. 'salt')** – In Shinto, salt is used for ritual purification of locations and people (harae, specifically *[shubatsu](/wiki/Harae#Practice "Harae")* ([修祓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BF%AE%E7%A5%93 "wiktionary:修祓"))), and small piles of salt are placed in dishes by the entrance of establishments for the two-fold purposes of warding off evil and attracting patrons.
* ***[Shinbutsu bunri](/wiki/Shinbutsu_bunri "Shinbutsu bunri")* ([神仏分離](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%BB%8F%E5%88%86%E9%9B%A2 "wiktionary:神仏分離"))** – The legal forbiddance of the [syncretism](/wiki/Syncretism "Syncretism") between Shinto and Buddhism.
* ***[Shiryō/shirei](/wiki/Shiry%C5%8D "Shiryō")* ([死霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%AD%BB%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:死霊"), lit. 'dead spirit')** – The spirit of a dead person. The terms are used in contrast to *ikiryō*, which refers to a disembodied spirit that leaves the body of a still-living person and haunts other people or places, sometimes across great distances.
* ***[Shintai](/wiki/Shintai "Shintai")* ([神体](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%BD%93 "wiktionary:神体"), lit. 'divine body')** – A sacred object, but also natural objects such as trees and mountains, which represents the *kami* for worship.
* **Shrine** – The English word that translates several more specialized Japanese words (see article [Shinto shrine](/wiki/Shinto_shrine#Shōgō "Shinto shrine")). Any structure housing a *kami*.
* ***[Shinbutsu kakuri](/wiki/Shinbutsu_kakuri "Shinbutsu kakuri")* ([神仏隔離](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%BB%8F%E9%9A%94%E9%9B%A2 "wiktionary:神仏隔離"))** – The tendency in medieval and early modern Japan to keep particular *kami* separate from any form or manifestation of Buddhism.
* ***[Shinbutsu shūgō](/wiki/Shinbutsu_sh%C5%ABg%C5%8D "Shinbutsu shūgō")* ([神仏習合](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E4%BB%8F%E7%BF%92%E5%90%88 "wiktionary:神仏習合"))** – The syncretism of Buddhism and local religious beliefs, the normal state of things before the *shinbutsu bunri*.
* ***[Shinsen](/wiki/Shinsen "Shinsen")* ([神饌](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9E%E9%A5%8C "wiktionary:神饌"))** – Offerings of foods given to Shinto shrines or *kamidana* for the *kami*.
* ***[Shio](/wiki/Salt#In_religion "Salt")* ([塩](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A1%A9 "wiktionary:塩"))** – In Shinto, [salt](/wiki/Salt "Salt") is used for offerings at Shinto shrines and a household's *kamidana*, [ritual purification](/wiki/Ritual_purification#Shinto "Ritual purification") of locations and people, and small piles of salt are placed in dishes by the entrance of establishments for the twofold purpose of warding off evil and attracting patrons.
* ***Shōdo* ([聖土](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%81%96%E5%9C%9F "wiktionary:聖土"), lit. 'Hallowed ground')** – See also *jōdo* (see above) and *seichi* (see above).
* ***[Shonetsu Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([焦熱地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%84%A6%E7%86%B1%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:焦熱地獄"), lit. 'Hell of Burning')** – The third level of *Jigoku*, where sinners who have committed murder, theft, and degeneration are sent.
* ***Shubatsu* ([修祓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BF%AE%E7%A5%93 "wiktionary:修祓"))** – A ceremony involving salt that is conducted immediately prior to rituals in order to purify participants, food offerings and *tamagushi*, of sins and defilement. See also *misogi*.
* ***[Shugenja](/wiki/Shugenja "Shugenja")* ([修験者](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BF%AE%E9%A8%93%E8%80%85 "wiktionary:修験者"))** – A practitioner of *shugendō*.
* ***[Shugo Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka_(Buddhism)#Hot_Narakas "Naraka (Buddhism)")* ([衆合地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%A1%86%E5%90%88%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:衆合地獄"))** – The sixth level of *Jigoku*, where sinners who have committed murder, theft, degeneration, drunkenness, lying, and blasphemy are sent.
* ***[Shuin](/wiki/Shuin "Shuin")* ([朱印](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0 "wiktionary:朱印"), lit. '[vermillion](/wiki/Vermillion "Vermillion") seal')** – A commemorative seal stamp given to worshippers and visitors to Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples in Japan. The seal stamps are often collected in *shuinchō* that are sold at shrines and temples.
* ***Shuinchō* ([朱印帳](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0%E5%B8%B3 "wiktionary:朱印帳"), lit. 'vermillion seal book')** – A book used to collect *shuin*.
* **[State Shinto](/wiki/State_Shinto "State Shinto")** – A term first used after World War II to broadly classify Shinto ideals, rituals and institutions created by the pre-war government to promote the divinity of the emperor and the uniqueness of Japan.
* ***[Shura](/wiki/Asura_(Buddhism) "Asura (Buddhism)")* ([修羅](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BF%AE%E7%BE%85 "wiktionary:修羅"))** – The semi-divine/Ashura realm of incarnation, the second-highest realm on the wheel of reincarnation. See also *rinne*.
* ***[Sōhei](/wiki/S%C5%8Dhei "Sōhei")* ([僧兵](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%83%A7%E5%85%B5 "wiktionary:僧兵"), lit. '[Buddhist] Monk Warrior')** – A *Sōhei* is a practitioner of *Shugendō*. *Sōhei* are also known as a *[Shugenja](/wiki/Shugenja "Shugenja")* (above), and a *[Yamabushi](/wiki/Yamabushi "Yamabushi")* (below); a kind of mountain [hermit](/wiki/Hermit "Hermit").
* ***[Sorei](/wiki/Sorei "Sorei")* ([祖霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%96%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:祖霊"), lit. 'ancestral spirits')** – A term in Shinto-Buddhism that refers to the [spirits of one's ancestors](/wiki/Veneration_of_the_dead#Japan "Veneration of the dead").
* ***Suikan* ([水干](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%B4%E5%B9%B2 "wiktionary:水干"))** – An informal garment, like a tunic, worn by males of the Japanese nobility in the Heian period, as outerwear; an informal garment, like a tunic, worn by males of the Japanese nobility in the Heian period, as outerwear.
* ***[Susanoo-no-Mikoto](/wiki/Susanoo-no-Mikoto "Susanoo-no-Mikoto")* ([須佐之男](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E4%B9%8B%E7%94%B7 "wiktionary:須佐之男") / [須佐能乎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E8%83%BD%E4%B9%8E "wiktionary:須佐能乎"))** – The multifaceted *kami* of storms, the sea, open fields, the harvest, marriage, and love, the son of Izanagi and the younger brother of Amaterasu Ōkami and Tsukuyomi.
* ***[Suzu](/wiki/Suzu_(bell) "Suzu (bell)")* ([鈴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%88%B4 "wiktionary:鈴"), lit. 'bell')** – A round, hollow Shinto bell that contains pellets that sound when agitated.

## T

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: T")]

* ***[Taiko](/wiki/Taiko "Taiko")* ([太鼓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%AA%E9%BC%93 "wiktionary:太鼓"), lit. 'great drum')** – A term for a broad range of Japanese percussion drums. The *taiko* drum has been an integral part of Japanese [Shinto](/wiki/Shinto "Shinto") and [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhism_in_Japan "Buddhism in Japan") religious practices and [folklore](/wiki/Japanese_folklore "Japanese folklore") for centuries.
* ***[Taisha](/wiki/Shinto_shrine "Shinto shrine")* ([大社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:大社"), lit. 'large shrine')** – A term usually used as a part of the official name of a shrine, as for example in [Izumo-taisha](/wiki/Izumo-taisha "Izumo-taisha").
* ***[Taisha-zukuri](/wiki/Taisha-zukuri "Taisha-zukuri")* ([大社造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A7%E7%A4%BE%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:大社造"), lit. 'large shrine architecture')** – The oldest style of shrine architecture used for example at Izumo-taisha.
* **[Takamagahara](/wiki/Takamagahara "Takamagahara")** ([高天原](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%8E%9F "wiktionary:高天原"), lit. 'Plains of the High Heaven') – In Shinto, the dwelling place of the heavenly gods, the *Amatsukami*. It is believed to be connected to the Earth by *Ama-no-uki-hashi*.
* ***[Takarabune](/wiki/Takarabune "Takarabune")* ([宝船](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%AE%9D%E8%88%B9 "wiktionary:宝船"), lit. 'treasure ship')** – A mythical ship piloted through the heavens by the Seven Lucky Gods during the first three days of the New Year.
* ***[Tamagaki](/wiki/Tamagaki "Tamagaki")* ([玉垣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8E%89%E5%9E%A3 "wiktionary:玉垣"), lit. 'jewel fence')** – The fence delimiting the sacred soil of a shrine.
* ***[Tamagushi](/wiki/Tamagushi "Tamagushi")\** ([玉串](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8E%89%E4%B8%B2 "wiktionary:玉串"), lit. 'jewel skewer')** – A form of offering made from a *sakaki*-tree branch and strips of paper, silk, or cotton.
* ***Tamashii* ([魂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%AD%82 "wiktionary:魂"))** – A soul within its proper body, encompassing one's mind, heart and soul. The opposite of *tamashii* is *mitama*.
* ***[Tamaya](/wiki/Tamaya "Tamaya")* ([霊屋](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%B1%8B "wiktionary:霊屋"), lit. 'spirit house')** – An altar used in Shinto-style ancestor worship, dedicated in the memory of deceased forebears. It generally has a mirror symbolizing the spirits of the deceased or a tablet bearing their names and is used not only to enshrine blood relatives, but also to honor respected non-family members..
* ***[Tanuki](/wiki/Japanese_raccoon_dog "Japanese raccoon dog")* ([狸](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8B%B8 "wiktionary:狸"))** – A mammal native to Japan (and Asia (as a subspecies of the [Asian raccoon dog](/wiki/Raccoon_dog "Raccoon dog"))) and to Japanese culture and folklore since ancient times. It is known in folklore to be mischievous and jolly, a master of disguise, and with a taste for *sake*. It is also regarded as a Japanese art animal, appearing in many different forms of both modern and traditional art.
* ***[Tanzaku](/wiki/Tanabata#Customs "Tanabata")* ([短冊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9F%AD%E5%86%8A "wiktionary:短冊"))** – A small strip of paper that wishes are written on, and is hung on bamboo or other trees during *[Tanabata](/wiki/Tanabata "Tanabata")*.
* ***Tatarigami* ([祟り神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A5%9F%E3%82%8A%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:祟り神"))** – Powerful evil spirits that bring calamity.
* ***Tate* ([盾](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9B%BE "wiktionary:盾"), lit. 'shield')** – A [shield](/wiki/Shield "Shield"), sometimes dedicated to shrines, and indicative of a *kami's* power to ward-off and protect from negative influences.
* ***[Tatsu](/wiki/Japanese_dragon "Japanese dragon")* ([竜](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AB%9C "wiktionary:竜")/[龍](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BE%8D "wiktionary:龍"), lit. 'Dragon')** – A mythical creature that is seen as a symbol for great power, wisdom, leadership and success, and is said to bring strength, luck, and fortune.
* ***[Tei](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([悌](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%82%8C "wiktionary:悌"), lit. '[fraternity](/wiki/Fraternity "Fraternity")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***[Temizuya](/wiki/Temizuya "Temizuya")\** ([手水舎](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E8%88%8E "wiktionary:手水舎"))** – A fountain near the entrance of a shrine or at a Buddhist temple where worshipers can cleanse their hands and mouths before worship.
* ***Tenchi* ([天地](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%9C%B0 "wiktionary:天地"), lit. 'heaven and earth')** – A term referring to the union of opposites.
* ***Tendō* ([天堂](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%A0%82 "wiktionary:天堂"), lit. 'heavenly hall')** – [Heaven](/wiki/Heaven "Heaven"), not unlike [nirvana](/wiki/Nirvana_(Buddhism) "Nirvana (Buddhism)"). Also known as *Tengoku*.
* ***[Tengu](/wiki/Tengu "Tengu")* ([天狗](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8B%97 "wiktionary:天狗"))** – A type of legendary creature found in Japanese folk religion. They are considered a type of *yōkai* or *kami*. The *tengu* were originally thought to take the forms of birds of prey, and they are traditionally depicted with both human and avian characteristics.
* ***Tengu-kakushi* ([天狗隠し](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8B%97%E9%9A%A0%E3%81%97 "wiktionary:天狗隠し"))** – A term used to refer to the mysterious disappearance or death of a person that happens when an angered *tengu* takes a person away.
* ***[Tenjin](/wiki/Tenjin_(kami) "Tenjin (kami)")* ([天神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:天神"), lit. 'sky god')** – The [deification](/wiki/Apotheosis "Apotheosis") of [Sugawara no Michizane](/wiki/Sugawara_no_Michizane "Sugawara no Michizane") (845–903). Originally worshiped as a [weather](/wiki/Weather "Weather") *kami*, Tenjin later became a [patron deity](/wiki/Patron_deity "Patron deity") of [academics](/wiki/Academy "Academy"), [scholarship](/wiki/Scholarship "Scholarship"), [learning](/wiki/Learning "Learning") and [education](/wiki/Education "Education"), and the [intelligentsia](/wiki/Intelligentsia "Intelligentsia").
* ***Tenkan* ([天冠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%86%A0 "wiktionary:天冠"), lit. 'heavenly crown')** – A design of golden-[filigree](/wiki/Filigree "Filigree") crown worn by Buddha and celestial beings, such as *tennyo*. Also worn by imperial princesses in the Heian period. Now worn by *miko* during formal occasions such as festivals.
* ***Tenne* ([纏衣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BA%8F%E8%A1%A3 "wiktionary:纏衣"), lit. a "Wrapping Raiment" or "Heavenly Raiment")** – Similar to the *hagoromo*.
* ***[Tennin](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin")* ([天人](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%BA%BA "wiktionary:天人"), lit. 'heavenly person')** – Divine beings found in Shinto and Japanese Buddhism that are similar to Western [angels](/wiki/Angel "Angel"), [nymphs](/wiki/Nymph "Nymph"), or [fairies](/wiki/Fairy "Fairy").
* ***[Tennyo](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin")* ([天女](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E5%A5%B3 "wiktionary:天女"), lit. 'heavenly woman')** – Female *tennin*.
* ***[Tensei/tenshō](/wiki/Reincarnation "Reincarnation")* ([転生](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%BB%A2%E7%94%9F "wiktionary:転生"))** – Reincarnation.
* ***[Tenshi](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin")* ([天使](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E4%BD%BF "wiktionary:天使"), lit. 'heavenly messenger')** – Messenger *tennin*.
* ***[Teru teru bōzu](/wiki/Teru_teru_b%C5%8Dzu "Teru teru bōzu")* ([照る照る坊主](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%85%A7%E3%82%8B%E7%85%A7%E3%82%8B%E5%9D%8A%E4%B8%BB "wiktionary:照る照る坊主"))** – A small traditional handmade doll made of white paper or cloth that Japanese farmers began hanging outside of their window by a string. This talisman is supposed to have magical powers to bring good weather and to stop or prevent a rainy day.
* **The [tide jewels](/wiki/Tide_jewels "Tide jewels")** – The *Kanju* and the *Manju*; two magical gems that the sea *kami* Watatsumi or Ryūjin used to control the tides.
* ***[Tokatsu Jigoku](/wiki/Naraka#Hot_Narakas "Naraka")* ([等活地獄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AD%89%E6%B4%BB%E5%9C%B0%E7%8D%84 "wiktionary:等活地獄"), lit. 'Hell of Revival')** – The first and shallowest level of *Jigoku*, where sinners who have committed murder are sent.
* ***[Torii](/wiki/Torii "Torii")* ([鳥居](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%B3%A5%E5%B1%85 "wiktionary:鳥居"))** – The Shinto gate at the entrance of a sacred area, usually a shrine. Also serves as the symbol for Shinto.
* ***[Tori-no-Ichi](/wiki/Kasai_Shrine#Tori_no_ichi "Kasai Shrine")* ([酉の市](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%85%89%E3%81%AE%E5%B8%82 "wiktionary:酉の市"), lit. 'market of the rooster')** – A festival in November.
* ***[Tōrō](/wiki/T%C5%8Dr%C5%8D "Tōrō")* ([灯籠](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%81%AF%E7%B1%A0 "wiktionary:灯籠"))** – A lantern at a shrine or Buddhist temple.
* ***Toshidana* ([年棚](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%B4%E6%A3%9A "wiktionary:年棚"), lit. 'Annum Shelf' or 'Annum Alter')** – An altar specifically used for the New-Year, to commemorate a *toshigami* (below). Offerings include round cakes made of pounded rice, bottles of *sake*, persimmons, tangerines, etc. See also *Kamidana* (above).
* **[Toshigami](/wiki/Toshigami "Toshigami")** – The *kami* of the year cycle.
* ***[Toso](/wiki/Toso "Toso")* ([屠蘇](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%A0%E8%98%87 "wiktionary:屠蘇"))** – A [spiced](/wiki/Spice "Spice") medicinal *sake*, traditionally drunk during New Year celebrations in Japan.
* ***[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi](/wiki/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi "Totsuka-no-Tsurugi")* ([十拳剣](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%81%E6%8B%B3%E5%89%A3 "wiktionary:十拳剣"))** – Any sword that is ten hand widths long. In Japanese mythology, numerous deities own a sword of this kind.
* ***Tsuina* ([追儺](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%BF%BD%E5%84%BA "wiktionary:追儺"))** – A type of ritual of exorcism, specifically one performed during a funeral, by a *hōsōshi* to keep away flesh-eating, corpse-stealing *yōkai* away from the body being buried. *Tsuina* shares its origins with *Setsubun* in *[Nuo](/wiki/Nuo_rituals "Nuo rituals")* rituals from China.
* ***[Tsukimi](/wiki/Tsukimi "Tsukimi")* ([月見](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%88%E8%A6%8B "wiktionary:月見"), lit. 'moon viewing')** – A festival honouring the autumn moon. The celebration of the [full moon](/wiki/Full_moon "Full moon") typically takes place on the 15th day of the eighth month of the traditional [Japanese calendar](/wiki/Japanese_calendar "Japanese calendar"); the [waxing moon](/wiki/Waxing_moon "Waxing moon") is celebrated on the 13th day of the ninth month.
* ***[Tsukubai](/wiki/Tsukubai "Tsukubai")* ([蹲踞](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%B9%B2%E8%B8%9E "wiktionary:蹲踞"))** – A [washbasin](/wiki/Washbasin "Washbasin") provided at the entrance to a holy place for visitors to purify themselves by the ritual washing of hands and rinsing of the mouth.
* ***[Tsukumogami](/wiki/Tsukumogami "Tsukumogami")* ([付喪神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%98%E5%96%AA%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:付喪神"))** – *yōkai* who came to being from tools that have acquired a *kami* or spirit. According to the *Ise Monogatari Shō*, there is a theory originally from the *Onmyōki* (陰陽記) that foxes and tanuki, among other beings, that have lived for at a hundred years and changed forms are considered tsukumogami.
* ***[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto](/wiki/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto "Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto")* ([月読命](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%88%E8%AA%AD%E5%91%BD "wiktionary:月読命") / [月読尊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%9C%88%E8%AA%AD%E5%B0%8A "wiktionary:月読尊"), lit. 'moon reader')** – The *kami* of the moon, the son of Izanagi, the younger brother of Amaterasu Ōkami, and the older brother of Susanoo-no-Mikoto.
* ***[Tsumairi](/wiki/Tsumairi "Tsumairi") *or* tsumairi-zukuri* ([妻入](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%BB%E5%85%A5 "wiktionary:妻入") or [妻入造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%BB%E5%85%A5%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:妻入造"), lit. 'Spouses Structure')** – A style of construction in which the building has its main entrance on the side which runs perpendicular to the roof's ridge (gabled side).
* ***[Tsumi](/wiki/Tsumi "Tsumi")* ([罪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%BD%AA "wiktionary:罪"), lit. 'sin')** – A violation committed against legal, social moral, ethical, or religious rules. It is most often used in the religious and moral sense. See also *amatsu tsumi*, *kunitsu tsumi*, *imi*, and *kegare*.
* ***[Tsuru](/wiki/Crane_(bird) "Crane (bird)")* ([鶴](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%B6%B4 "wiktionary:鶴"), lit. 'crane')** – A species of bird that is native to Japan and to Japanese culture.
* ****Tsurushi-bina** ([吊るし雛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%90%8A%E3%82%8B%E3%81%97%E9%9B%9B "wiktionary:吊るし雛")/[つるし雛](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8B%E3%81%97%E9%9B%9B "wiktionary:つるし雛"), lit. 'Hanging Dolls')** – Strings of hanging dolls, and all manner of [auspicious](/wiki/Luck "Luck") symbols (cranes, turtles, etc.), that are a traditional decoration, like a [hanging mobile](/wiki/Hanging_mobile "Hanging mobile"), for *Hinamatsuri*. Also known as **Sagemon** ([さげもん](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%95%E3%81%92%E3%82%82%E3%82%93 "wiktionary:さげもん"), lit. '"Hanging Thing"'), **Sagarimono** ([さがりもの](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%95%E3%81%8C%E3%82%8A%E3%82%82%E3%81%AE "wiktionary:さがりもの"), lit. '"Hanging Thing"'), **Tsurushi kazari** ([つるしかざり](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8B%E3%81%97%E3%81%8B%E3%81%96%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:つるしかざり"), lit. '"Hanging Decoration/Wreath"') and **Kasafuku** ([傘福](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%82%98%E7%A6%8F "wiktionary:傘福"), lit. '"Umbrella [of] good luck"')

## U

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: U")]

* ***Ubusunagami* ([産土神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:産土神"), lit. 'birthplace deity')** – A type of tutelary deity, a guardian god or spirit of a particular place in Shinto. The term *ubusuna-mode* (visiting one's own birth god) became widely used for visiting one's hometown and shrine. See also *sore* and *ujigami*.
* ***[Uchide no kozuchi](/wiki/Uchide_no_kozuchi "Uchide no kozuchi")* ([打ち出の小槌](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%89%93%E3%81%A1%E5%87%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%B0%8F%E6%A7%8C "wiktionary:打ち出の小槌"))** – Daikokuten's magic, lucky coin-stamping mallet.
* ***[Ujigami](/wiki/Ujigami "Ujigami")* ([氏神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%8F%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:氏神"), lit. 'clan deity')** – A [guardian](/wiki/Tutelary_deity "Tutelary deity") god or spirit of a particular place in Shinto.
* ***Ukehi *or* Ukei* ([誓占](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%AA%93%E5%8D%A0 "wiktionary:誓占"))** – A Shinto [divination](/wiki/Divination "Divination") ritual.
* ***[Ukemochi](/wiki/Ukemochi "Ukemochi")* ([保食神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BF%9D%E9%A3%9F%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:保食神"), lit. 'sustainable food deity')** – The *kami* of food.
* ***[Ushi no toki mairi](/wiki/Ushi_no_toki_mairi "Ushi no toki mairi")* ([丑の時参り](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%B8%91%E3%81%AE%E6%99%82%E5%8F%82%E3%82%8A "wiktionary:丑の時参り"), lit. 'visit of the hour of the ox')** – 1:00 am–3:00 am; the equivalent of the [witching hour](/wiki/Witching_hour "Witching hour").
* ***Utsushimi* ([現身](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8F%BE%E8%BA%AB "wiktionary:現身"), lit. 'existing body')** – A term for a visible, physical form (of a deity).
* ***Utsushiyo* ([現世](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%8F%BE%E4%B8%96 "wiktionary:現世"), lit. 'existing world')** – A term for a visible, physical world.

## W

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: W")]

* ***Waraningyō* ([藁人形](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E8%97%81%E4%BA%BA%E5%BD%A2 "wiktionary:藁人形"))** – Depending-upon their intended use, *Wara-Ningyō* can be used either for cleansing a person of sins and impurities, casting spells to protect or empower, or inflict a curse upon another person via ritual impalement. Originating from Taoist rituals, they are nailed to trees to curse people, during *ushi no toki mairi*.

## Y

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Y")]

* ***Yaku* ([厄](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8E%84 "wiktionary:厄"), lit. 'misfortune')** – The bad luck that one accumulates every day.
* ***Yakudoshi* ([厄年](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8E%84%E5%B9%B4 "wiktionary:厄年"), lit. 'year of misfortune')** – A period of time where a person experiences their own "age of calamity", where they will suffer misfortunes.
* ***[Yama](/wiki/Yama "Yama")* ([夜摩](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A4%9C%E6%91%A9 "wiktionary:夜摩"))** – Another name for *[Enma-Ō](/wiki/Yama_(Buddhism) "Yama (Buddhism)")* ([閻魔王](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%96%BB%E9%AD%94%E7%8E%8B "wiktionary:閻魔王"), lit. 'Evil Gates King'), the *kami* who oversees *[Naraku](/wiki/Naraka "Naraka")*.
* ***[Yamabushi](/wiki/Yamabushi "Yamabushi")* ([山伏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%B1%E4%BC%8F "wiktionary:山伏"))** – A practitioner of *shugendō*.
* ***[Yamajijii](/wiki/Yamajijii "Yamajijii")* ([山爺](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%B1%E7%88%BA "wiktionary:山爺"))** – A male *yōkai* found in Japanese folklore and Noh.
* ***[Yama-uba](/wiki/Yama-uba "Yama-uba")* ([山姥](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%B1%E5%A7%A5 "wiktionary:山姥"), lit. 'mountain crone')** – A female *yōkai* found in Japanese folklore and Noh, and is most often described as a monstrous crone.
* ***[Yamawaro](/wiki/Yamawaro "Yamawaro")* ([山童](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%B1%E7%AB%A5 "wiktionary:山童"), lit. 'Mountain Child')** – A childlike *yōkai* found in Japanese folklore and Noh.
* ***Yaoyorozu no kami*** ([八百万の神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E7%99%BE%E4%B8%87%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:八百万の神"), lit. 'eight million *kami*') – All *kami*.
* ***[Yata no Kagami](/wiki/Yata_no_Kagami "Yata no Kagami")*** ([八咫鏡](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E5%92%AB%E9%8F%A1 "wiktionary:八咫鏡")) – A sacred mirror that is part of the Imperial Regalia of Japan. It is said to be housed in Ise Grand Shrine in Mie Prefecture, Japan. The *Yata no Kagami* represents wisdom or honesty, depending on the source.
* ***[Yari](/wiki/Yari "Yari")* ([槍](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%A7%8D "wiktionary:槍"), lit. 'spear')** – A spear, sometimes dedicated to shrines (along with other weapons, such as *Katana* and *Naginata*), and indicative of a *kami*'s power to ward-off negative influences.
* ***Yashikigami* ([屋敷神](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B1%8B%E6%95%B7%E7%A5%9E "wiktionary:屋敷神"))** – A *kami* of a specific plot of land, house, household, etc.
* ***Yashiro* ([社](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%A4%BE "wiktionary:社"))** – A generic term for *shrine*, similar to *jinja*.
* ***[Yōkai](/wiki/Y%C5%8Dkai "Yōkai")* ([妖怪](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%96%E6%80%AA "wiktionary:妖怪"))** – An umbrella term that covers many supernatural beings, *Yōkai* can also be used when referring to humans, animals, objects, and even *kami*.
* ***Yōma* ([妖魔](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A6%96%E9%AD%94 "wiktionary:妖魔"))** – A type of ghost, phantom, apparition, monster, or demon.
* **[Yomi](/wiki/Yomi "Yomi")** ([黄泉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%BB%84%E6%B3%89 "wiktionary:黄泉"), lit. 'Underworld') – The land of the dead; the afterworld, the underworld: according to Shinto mythology, as related in *Kojiki*, it is where the dead go in the afterlife.
* ***[Yorishiro](/wiki/Yorishiro "Yorishiro")* ([依り代](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BE%9D%E3%82%8A%E4%BB%A3 "wiktionary:依り代"))** – An object capable of attracting *kami* for a ceremony. Trees, rocks, *magatama*, *gohei*, and people can be *yorishiro*.
* ***Yorimashi* ([憑坐](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%86%91%E5%9D%90 "wiktionary:憑坐"))** – A human *yorishiro*, in particular a child or woman, used by a faith healer for oracles.
* ***Yoroi* ([鎧](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8E%A7 "wiktionary:鎧"), lit. 'armour')** – A suit of armour, sometimes dedicated to shrines, and indicative of a *kami*'s power to protect from negative influences.
* ***[Yū](/wiki/Bushido#Eight_virtues_of_bushido_(as_defined_by_Nitobe_Inazō) "Bushido")* ([勇](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8B%87%E6%B0%97 "wiktionary:勇気"), lit. '[courage](/wiki/Courage "Courage")')** – One of the virtues of *bushido*.
* ***Yūki* ([幽鬼](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%BD%E9%AC%BC "wiktionary:幽鬼"))** – A type of demonic ghost or phantom; a demonic [poltergeist](/wiki/Poltergeist "Poltergeist"). An *[onryō](/wiki/Onry%C5%8D "Onryō")* is a kind of *yūki*.
* ***[Yūrei](/wiki/Y%C5%ABrei "Yūrei")* ([幽霊](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%B9%BD%E9%9C%8A "wiktionary:幽霊"))** – A type of [ghost](/wiki/Ghost "Ghost") or phantom. An *[onryō](/wiki/Onry%C5%8D "Onryō")* is a kind of *yūrei*.

## Z

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glossary_of_Shinto&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Z")]

* ***[Zōni](/wiki/Z%C5%8Dni "Zōni")* ([雑煮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9B%91%E7%85%AE "wiktionary:雑煮"))** – *A* Japanese soup containing *mochi* rice cakes; associated with the Japanese New Year and its tradition of *Osechi* ceremonial foods.
* ***[Zuijin](/wiki/Zuijin "Zuijin")* ([随身](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9A%8F%E8%BA%AB "wiktionary:随身"))** – *Kami* warrior-guardian figures. They are often depicted to be holding bows and arrows, or wearing three silver rings. The name was originally applied to the bodyguards of the Emperor of Japan.
* ***[Zukuri](/wiki/Shinto_architecture "Shinto architecture")* ([造](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%80%A0 "wiktionary:造"), lit. 'architecture')** – A style of construction a building, that a shrine has usually been built in.
